tasks.title: Core Models and Abstract Interfaces Setup
tasks.description: Define the core normalized UsageEvent Pydantic model and abstract interfaces (UsageSource, LimitsSource, PricingStrategy) for the shared core package (tokemetry_core). Implement the FakeProvider test double to validate interfaces without Claude-specific assumptions.
tasks.details: Core package implemented: `models.py` includes `UsageEvent`, `DailyAggregate`, `LimitSnapshot`, `SourceFile`, `ParseResult`, `PriceRow`, and `Provenance` enum; all models are frozen Pydantic v2 with tz-aware timestamp validation and non-negative counts. `interfaces.py` defines `UsageSource`, `LimitsSource`, `PricingStrategy` ABCs plus `LimitsUnavailableError` for graceful degradation. `registry.py` implements `ProviderRegistry` with factories for sources and instances for pricing. `providers/fake.py` contains deterministic `FakeProvider` as reference implementation. Design deviations documented in `docs/architecture/core-package.md`: `parse` returns `ParseResult` (events + new_offset atomically) instead of iterator; `poll` returns list of snapshots (multi-window). Gates: 31 tests pass, coverage 99%, ruff/mypy strict/trivy clean.

tasks.title: Anthropic Pricing Strategy and LiteLLM Sync
tasks.description: Implement the Anthropic pricing strategy with date-versioned per-MTok rates per provider/model, synced from LiteLLM's model_prices_and_context_window.json. Add manual override support and handle dated/undated model IDs.
tasks.details: The pricing subpackage is now implemented: `litellm.py` (pure transform of LiteLLM price db to per-MTok PriceRows, skips platform-prefixed aliases, fallback multipliers 1.25x/2x/0.1x when cache rates missing), `table.py` (PricingTable with date-versioned resolution, dated/undated model id matching both directions, apply_overrides for TOML manual overrides with field validation, UnknownModelError), and `anthropic.py` (AnthropicPricingStrategy linear dot product quantized to micro-USD, DEFAULT_ANTHROPIC_PRICE_ROWS verified snapshot). `providers/anthropic.py` registers the strategy. HTTP fetching is deliberately kept out of core - the server owns it. Docs: `docs/architecture/pricing.md`. Gates: 51 tests, 98.85% coverage, ruff/mypy strict/trivy clean. Create `packages/core/tokemetry_core/pricing/anthropic.py` implementing `PricingStrategy` (or verify the existing implementation matches the spec). Fetch `model_prices_and_context_window.json` from LiteLLM via the server, parse into a date-versioned pricing table using `table.py`. Implement logic to resolve price for dated (e.g., `claude-opus-4-5-20251101`) and undated (e.g., `claude-fable-5`) model IDs using `table.py`'s matching. Add `manual_override` config in TOML to adjust prices using `table.py`'s `apply_overrides`. Compute cost using Anthropic formula: uncached input * input_price + 5m_cache * 1.25x + 1h_cache * 2x + cache_read * 0.1x + output * output_price. Add unit tests for price resolution and cost calculation.
tasks.testStrategy: Test price resolution for dated and undated models with fixtures. Verify cost calculation matches Anthropic formula. Confirm manual override updates pricing table. Run pytest with coverage >= 80%. Validate ruff/mypy strict/trivy zero issues.

tasks.title: Claude Code JSONL Parser with Keep-Max Dedup
tasks.description: Implement the Claude Code JSONL parser (ClaudeCodeJsonlSource) to parse ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/*.jsonl and subagent transcripts. Extract usage fields and implement keep-max deduplication by requestId.
tasks.details: Created `providers/claude_code.py` implementing `UsageSource`. discover() uses rglobs to find projects/**/*.jsonl including subagents, honoring CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR. parse() performs binary-reads from byte offset, implements keep-max dedup by requestId (largest output_tokens wins per pass), splits cache TTL into 5m/1h (short/long) with legacy fallback, aggregates server_tool_use counters into extra, skips non-assistant/synthetic/no-usage records, counts malformed lines (not silent drop), and leaves incomplete trailing line unconsumed. bootstrap() imports stats-cache.json dailyModelTokens as DailyAggregate. tasks.title: Idempotent Ingest Endpoints with Validation
tasks.description: Implement idempotent ingest endpoints (POST /api/v1/ingest/events, /ingest/limits, /ingest/bootstrap) with batch all-or-nothing logic, upsert keeping maximum-usage row per event_id, and sanity validation rejecting garbage.
tasks.details: The ingest layer is already implemented. Update `apps/server/tokemetry_server/api/ingest.py` to integrate with the existing FastAPI app factory (app.py) where lifespan runs Alembic upgrade and wires engine/session_factory/dialect/cost_fn on app.state. Ensure injectable cost_fn defaults to no-op per task split. Wire models in `api/schemas.py` (EventsIngest/LimitsIngest/BootstrapIngest) with to_core converters, extra=forbid, and batch size bounds. Integrate bearer auth from `api/auth.py` (bootstrap token constant-time + hashed api_tokens lookup refreshing last_used). Use `api/deps.py` for session + ingest service deps. The 3 idempotent routes in `api/ingest.py` should return 400 on validation errors and 422 on schema errors. In `services/ingest.py`, ensure dedup keeps max usage before upsert, includes machine touch, handles events keep-max upsert, limits append, and bootstrap rollup upsert. Use `services/validation.py` for viberank-style sanity checks (token max 10B, clock skew 2h). Implement dialect-aware ON CONFLICT keep-max in `db/upsert.py` for PostgreSQL+SQLite via DialectInsert union. Ensure `security.py` uses SHA-256 token hash + generate. Add daily_rollups.total_tokens+provenance columns (amended migration+ORM). Store cost_usd as NULL pending cost engine. Update docs in `docs/api/ingest.md`. Ensure 91 tests pass, ruff/mypy strict/trivy clean, and no warnings.

tasks.title: AnthropicOAuthLimitsSource Poller with Graceful Degradation
tasks.description: Implement the AnthropicOAuthLimitsSource poller to poll the undocumented endpoint GET https://api.anthropic.com/api/oauth/usage with OAuth token from ~/.claude/.credentials.json. Add graceful degradation to local estimates on failure.
tasks.details: AnthropicOAuthLimitsSource is implemented (collector-side to keep core httpx-free). Reads OAuth token from ~/.claude/.credentials.json using defensive recursive accessToken search. GET /api/oauth/usage with Bearer + `anthropic-beta: oauth-2025-04-20` + Claude-Code UA. Maps five_hour/seven_day/seven_day_opus/seven_day_sonnet windows to normalized LimitSnapshots (provenance=official, resets_at parses ISO or epoch). Skips null windows. Token never leaves machine. tasks.title: Cache-read off by default, selection-aware percent, sticky selection
tasks.description: EChart emits legendselectchanged (chart.on 'legendselectchanged') because notMerge wipes selection each render. stackedTokenBarOption/stackedAreaOption accept a selected map → set legend.selected AND compute the normalized denominator from only visible components, so hiding cache-read re-normalizes the rest to 100%. Views hold a selection ref (default {'cache read': false}), feed it to builders (auto-refresh preserves it), update from the legend event, and persist per-view via loadSelection/saveSelection in useSettings (localStorage). Cache-read is OFF by default on every composition chart (Now model, Trends daily+dimension, Breakdowns 3 stacks). Unit test covers the re-normalize math. 70 dashboard tests pass; build+lint clean.
tasks.details: Implementation: 1) Set cache-read OFF by default on all composition charts. 2) Wire EChart legendselectchanged event to update view selection ref. 3) Pass selected map to stacked builders; builders set legend.selected and compute normalized percent over visible components only. 4) Views persist selection per-view using loadSelection/saveSelection in useSettings (localStorage). 5) Auto-refresh feeds existing selection ref to builders. 6) Unit test validates re-normalize math. Verification: 70 dashboard tests pass; build+lint+format clean.

Objective: canonical registries so every later epic resolves providers and models centrally (Migration Phase 1; no v1 behavior change).

Design: providers table (id lowercase stable, display_name, aliases JSON, pricing_strategy, limit_semantics, supported_dimensions, registered flag); models table with PK (provider, native_model_id), lifecycle active/deprecated/retired/unknown, capabilities JSON, first_seen/last_seen; model_aliases table (provider, alias) unique mapping to native_model_id with rule_version. Alias normalization is centralized in packages/core (z.ai and zai and legacy names normalize to zai; existing Claude Code provider id anthropic remains valid unchanged per FR-PROVIDER-009). Native model IDs are always retained exactly as reported (FR-MODEL-001); pricing lookups use provider plus native model plus effective date (FR-MODEL-003). Unknown providers may be ingested when policy permits but are marked unregistered and produce data_quality_events records (FR-PROVIDER-005, FR-MODEL-006). Provider registration must not require dashboard code changes (FR-PROVIDER-007). Seed anthropic, openai, zai (FR-PROVIDER-008) plus the fake test provider.

Acceptance criteria (PRD Epic TOK-2): Anthropic, OpenAI, and Z.ai registered; aliases normalize centrally; model IDs remain native and provider-scoped; unknown provider and model data quality records generated; existing Claude data remains queryable.

tasks.title: TOK-3: Usage Event v2 Ledger and Ingest API v2
tasks.description: Add the v2 attempt-event lifecycle (finality, sequence, revisions, corrections), the new usage_events_v2 ledger with v1 backfill and compatibility view, logical_requests, privacy validation, and the /api/v2/ingest endpoints while keeping v1 endpoints wire-identical. PRD-TOK-002 Sections 11, 12.3, 12.4, 12.5; Epic TOK-3; decision D-001.
tasks.details:
Objective: provider-neutral event lifecycle and ingest (Migration Phase 2, v2 behind the api surface only; existing collectors stay on v1).

Design decisions: new usage_events_v2 table rather than in-place evolution (D-001); usage_event_revisions for superseded and conflicting states and corrections; logical_requests dedicated table (D-003); event identity (provider, event_id) with event_kind attempt|logical_request|import|adjustment, finality snapshot|final, increasing sequence; final supersedes snapshot; final-to-final requires admin correction (D-002); same-sequence conflicts rejected to data_quality_events (FR-IDEMP-008); replay is a no-op (FR-IDEMP-007); batches transactional (FR-IDEMP-009). V1 ingest maps into v2 rows with event_kind attempt and finality final, applying legacy keep-max-output as documented compatibility conflict mode (FR-IDEMP-012, FR-EVENT-023). After verified backfill the physical usage_events table is replaced by a read-only v1-shaped compatibility view. Privacy: content fields rejected (FR-EVENT-021/022, FR-PRIV-001/002), dimensions allowlist with bounds 16 keys, 64-char keys, 256-char values (D-004), tool-name histogram accepted only behind server-side opt-in and default off (D-005), maximum event size and JSON depth enforced (FR-EVENT-028, NFR-SEC-004).

Endpoints: POST /api/v2/ingest/events, /api/v2/ingest/limits, /api/v2/ingest/aggregates, /api/v2/ingest/validate (schema plus semantic checks without persistence, FR-INGEST-007), GET /api/v2/schemas/usage-event. Structured validation errors with event index, field path, code, message (FR-INGEST-006); server batch IDs and ingest_batches table (FR-INGEST-008); request IDs on every response (FR-INGEST-016); gzip support (FR-INGEST-010); OpenAPI for all v2 schemas (FR-INGEST-012).

Acceptance criteria (PRD Epic TOK-3): v2 schema and OpenAPI published; attempts, logical request IDs, finality, sequence, outcomes, source, routing, and reasoning supported; v1 events map into internal v2 representation; v1 endpoints retain behavior; validation endpoint exists; batch tests cover duplicates, snapshots, finality, and conflicts.

Test strategy: unit tests for v1-to-v2 conversion, validation, snapshot and finality resolution, conflict and correction behavior; integration tests for duplicate batches, snapshot followed by final, conflicting same-sequence events; golden v1 fixtures from Task 60 must pass unchanged; migration tests on SQLite and Postgres including the backfill count and sum verification; performance tests for 100-event batch p95 under 200 ms and a 5000-event compatibility batch.

subtasks.title: Core v2 wire model and published JSON schema
subtasks.description: Define the complete v2 usage event wire model in packages/core with a machine-readable JSON schema.
subtasks.details: Create packages/core/src/tokemetry_core/models_v2.py (or usage_v2.py) with frozen extra=forbid pydantic models implementing the full PRD 12.3 wire shape: schema_version literal 2 (FR-EVENT-001); event_id; event_kind attempt|logical_request|import|adjustment (FR-EVENT-003); finality snapshot|final (FR-EVENT-005); sequence int >= 0 (FR-EVENT-006); logical_request_id, attempt_id, provider_request_id, provider_response_id (FR-EVENT-009..011); provider; requested_model, routed_model, native_model as separate fields (FR-EVENT-012); ts_started, ts_first_token, ts_completed all timezone-aware (FR-EVENT-013, reuse the tz validator pattern from models.py); machine, project, session_id, agent_id, environment; six non-negative token counters input, output, cache_read, cache_write_short, cache_write_long, reasoning (FR-EVENT-014/015); success bool plus outcome string kept separate (FR-EVENT-017); http_status, stop_reason, service_tier, streaming, latency_ms, time_to_first_token_ms, tool_call_count, optional tool histogram dict; provenance official|local_estimate|stats_cache|imported|adjusted (FR-EVENT-025, extending the existing Provenance enum); source object with type, name, version, optional instance_id (FR-EVENT-018); optional gateway-neutral routing object (FR-EVENT-019); bounded dimensions dict (FR-EVENT-020); namespaced extra dict (FR-PROVIDER-006); trace_id, span_id, parent_span_id (FR-OTEL-001). Export a JSON schema document for GET /api/v2/schemas/usage-event. No content fields exist in the schema at all (FR-EVENT-021). Test strategy: unit tests for every field bound, tz enforcement, non-negative counters, kind and finality literals, zero-token failed attempts accepted (FR-EVENT-024), and JSON schema generation stability (snapshot).

subtasks.title: Privacy validation layer with prohibited-key fuzzing
subtasks.description: Server-side strict privacy enforcement for all v2 metadata.
subtasks.details: Add services/privacy.py implementing FR-EVENT-022 and FR-PRIV-001/002: a prohibited-key detector applied recursively to extra, dimensions, routing, and the tool histogram, rejecting or stripping (strict server policy setting privacy_mode reject|strip, default reject) keys that look like content (prompt, response, message, content, text, input_text, output_text, arguments, file_path, path, code, snippet, reasoning_content and variants); dimension allowlist enforcement per decision D-004 (configurable, defaults team, cost_center, environment; bounds 16 keys, key length 64, value length 256 per FR-EVENT-020); tool-name histogram gate per D-005 (server setting tool_names_enabled default false; when disabled any histogram is rejected; when enabled entries bounded to 32 names of 64 chars with integer counts); extra namespace bounding (top-level keys must be provider ids or gateway, size-capped); maximum serialized event size (default 32 KiB, configurable per FR-EVENT-028) and JSON depth limit (NFR-SEC-004). Violations produce structured field-path errors for the ingest error format. Test strategy: unit tests per rule; property-based prohibited-key fuzz tests (hypothesis) generating nested payloads with forbidden keys at random depths asserting rejection (FR-PRIV-012, AC-011, AC-022); performance sanity that validation of a 100-event batch stays well under the ingest budget.

subtasks.title: Ledger tables migration: usage_events_v2, usage_event_revisions, logical_requests
subtasks.description: Create the three lifecycle tables with indexes on both engines.
subtasks.details: Alembic migration creating: usage_events_v2 with composite PK (provider, event_id) and all wire fields flattened per the design spec section 3.1 (six BigInteger token counters, Numeric latencies as integers ms, JSONType for routing, dimensions, extra, tool_histogram; schema_version int; source_id FK-less integer reference until Task 63 lands, then FK; trace/span/parent ids as indexed strings); usage_event_revisions (id autoincrement, provider, event_id, sequence, finality, payload JSONType snapshot of the superseded state, reason superseded|conflict|correction, actor, ts) with index on (provider, event_id); logical_requests with PK (provider, logical_request_id), requested_model, session_id, routing_policy, routing_reason, attempt_count, fallback_count, winning_attempt_id, ts_first, ts_last. Indexes on usage_events_v2: ts_started, machine, session_id, native_model, logical_request_id, source_id, provider_request_id (FR-EVENT-011), outcome. Follow db/base.py conventions; keep the existing usage_events table untouched in this migration (the swap happens in subtask 10). Test strategy: upgrade and downgrade tests on SQLite and Postgres; index existence assertions; insert round-trip for every column including JSON payloads on both engines.

subtasks.title: Revision engine: finality, sequence, conflicts, corrections
subtasks.description: Implement the event lifecycle resolution rules that decide whether an incoming event inserts, supersedes, duplicates, conflicts, or corrects.
subtasks.details: Add services/revisions.py implementing the PRD 12.4 state machine for (provider, event_id): new id inserts; snapshot over snapshot with higher sequence supersedes (previous state archived to usage_event_revisions with reason superseded, FR-IDEMP-002/003); final supersedes any snapshot (FR-IDEMP-004); identical replay is a byte-equal no-op returning duplicate (FR-IDEMP-007); same sequence with differing payload rejects and records a sequence_conflict data quality event (FR-IDEMP-008); later snapshot never supersedes a final (FR-EVENT-008); final over final requires the correction flag plus admin:corrections scope and archives the previous state with reason correction, actor, timestamp, and reason text (FR-IDEMP-005/006, FR-EVENT-026, decision D-002). Legacy keep-max compatibility mode: events entering through the v1 mapper resolve conflicts by output_tokens >= comparison exactly as db/upsert.py does today (FR-IDEMP-012), implemented as an explicit conflict-mode enum so the behavior is documented and testable rather than accidental. Returns a per-event outcome (accepted, updated, duplicate, rejected, corrected) consumed by the ingest service. Test strategy: exhaustive unit matrix over (existing state x incoming kind/finality/sequence) including replay determinism; correction audit content assertions; keep-max mode equivalence tests against fixtures derived from the current upsert behavior.

subtasks.title: Ingest service v2 with transactional batches and ingest_batches ledger
subtasks.description: Batch orchestration, per-event outcome counts, batch IDs, request IDs.
subtasks.details: Add services/ingest_v2.py: accepts a validated batch, runs privacy validation (subtask 2) then the revision engine (subtask 4) per event inside one transaction (FR-IDEMP-009, NFR-REL-001); on any structural failure the batch rolls back atomically; response carries a server-generated batch id (FR-INGEST-008), counts for accepted, updated, duplicate, rejected, corrected (FR-IDEMP-011), and optionally the accepted and updated event ids when the client requests them subject to response-size limits (FR-INGEST-009). Create ingest_batches table via migration (batch_id, source identity, token label, schema_version, counts, received_at, request_id) retained 30 days per retention defaults. Add request-id middleware stamping every API response (FR-INGEST-016) if not present. WebSocket publication after commit reuses the existing broadcaster and must never roll back accepted ingest (NFR-REL-008). Ingest logs never contain bearer tokens or event metadata values (FR-INGEST-017). Test strategy: integration tests for transactional all-or-nothing behavior, count correctness across mixed batches (new, replay, snapshot upgrade, conflict), batch ledger rows, and WS publish failure isolation; unit tests for response-size limiting of returned id lists.

subtasks.title: Ingest endpoints: POST /api/v2/ingest/events and /api/v2/ingest/validate
subtasks.description: The v2 ingest HTTP surface with structured errors, gzip, and configurable limits.
subtasks.details: Add api/v2/ingest.py: POST /api/v2/ingest/events accepting a batch envelope (schema_version, source object, events list) with configurable maximum event count and byte size (FR-INGEST-005, defaults 1000 events and 5 MiB, settings-driven); gzip request body support (FR-INGEST-010); bearer auth (FR-INGEST-001) using require_token now and ingest:events scope once Task 63 lands; validation failures return the structured error list with event index, field path, code, and message (FR-INGEST-006) and reject the batch. POST /api/v2/ingest/validate runs schema plus semantic plus privacy checks and returns the same error structure without persisting anything (FR-INGEST-007, FR-IDEMP-010) so exporters can pre-flight batches. Health and readiness endpoints stay unauthenticated and readiness reports database plus migration status without secrets (FR-INGEST-018/019). Rate limiting distinguishes ingest from query traffic (FR-INGEST-015) via a simple in-process token bucket configurable per class, hardened further in Task 70. Test strategy: integration tests for happy path, oversized batch, malformed event index reporting, gzip round trip, validate-endpoint non-persistence (row counts unchanged), unauthenticated health, and rate-limit classes; error-format snapshot test used later by client generators.

subtasks.title: V2 limits and aggregates ingest endpoints
subtasks.description: POST /api/v2/ingest/limits and /api/v2/ingest/aggregates with source identity.
subtasks.details: Add POST /api/v2/ingest/limits accepting provider-neutral limit snapshots carrying the v2 dimensions (provider, window_kind opaque, account, organization, machine, source identity, utilization, remaining, limit_amount, unit, resets_at, provenance official|estimated per FR-LIMIT-002..004) and POST /api/v2/ingest/aggregates for historical importer bootstraps (daily aggregate rows with provenance imported, mapping onto the rollup upsert path the way v1 bootstrap does today). Both share the batch envelope, structured errors, gzip, request ids, and transactional semantics from subtask 6, with scopes ingest:limits and ingest:aggregates respectively once Task 63 lands. Limit snapshots remain append-only. The limit_snapshots dimension columns themselves are added in Task 69 subtask 2; until that lands the endpoint validates the wire shape and stores what the current table supports, feature-flagging the extended dimensions (document the flag in the task log). Test strategy: integration tests for both endpoints covering append-only behavior, provenance labeling, batch bounds from schemas, and scope placeholders; wire-schema unit tests; replay safety (identical snapshot batches do not duplicate beyond append semantics documented for v1 parity).

subtasks.title: V1-to-v2 backfill migration with count and sum verification
subtasks.description: Copy all historical v1 events into the v2 ledger and prove equality before any cutover.
subtasks.details: Write the backfill as an Alembic data migration plus a standalone verification tool. Backfill: every usage_events row becomes a usage_events_v2 row with event_kind attempt, finality final, sequence 0, provenance preserved, native_model from the v1 model column, requested_model and routed_model null, source_id null (attributed later by Task 63 subtask 6), all six token counters copied (reasoning 0), extra JSON preserved, machine, project, session_id, ts columns mapped onto ts_started (v1 has one timestamp; ts_completed set equal, latency null) per FR-EVENT-023. Batched copy (10000 rows per chunk) so SQLite and Postgres both survive production-scale tables; resumable via high-water mark; never mutates source rows. Verification tool (CLI, also runnable via admin endpoint): per day, provider, and machine compare row counts and sums of all five v1 token columns plus cost between usage_events and usage_events_v2, emitting a machine-readable report; any mismatch blocks subtasks 9 and 10. Downgrade path drops v2 rows originating from backfill (marked via provenance-preserving batch tag in extra or a backfill marker column). Test strategy: migration tests seeding representative v1 data (including duplicates already collapsed, null projects, unknown-model rows with null cost) then asserting the verification report is all-equal; interrupted-backfill resume test; downgrade test restoring the pre-backfill state.

subtasks.title: Repoint v1 ingest and server services to the v2 ledger
subtasks.description: All reads and writes go through usage_events_v2; v1 endpoints stay wire-identical.
subtasks.details: Switch the server to the v2 ledger: the v1 ingest path (api/ingest.py, services/ingest.py) maps EventIn to a v2 event (event_kind attempt, finality final, keep-max compatibility conflict mode) and writes through the revision engine; queries in services/queries.py, analytics.py, insights.py, session_stats.py, report.py, and rollups.py read from usage_events_v2; cost stamping moves to the computed_costs flow only when Task 64 lands, so in this subtask the existing cost_usd semantics are preserved by writing cost into the v2 row-compatible location the view exposes (transitional column, removed in Task 64 subtask 4 cutover; document in task log). The golden v1 fixture suite from 60.4 is the acceptance gate: every v1 ingest and query response must be byte-identical. In-batch dedupe semantics (accepted plus duplicates_merged counts) must match current behavior exactly. Test strategy: full existing pytest suite green with reads/writes on the v2 ledger; golden suite unchanged; targeted tests that v1 duplicate handling produces identical stored rows as before (fixture comparison against 62.4 keep-max tests).

subtasks.title: Replace usage_events with the v1 compatibility view
subtasks.description: Swap the physical v1 table for a read-only view projecting v2 data in the v1 column shape.
subtasks.details: Alembic migration that, only after the verification report from subtask 8 passes (enforced by a migration pre-check that runs the verifier), renames usage_events to usage_events_v1_archive and creates a view named usage_events selecting the v1 columns from usage_events_v2 (provider, event_id, machine, session_id, ts from ts_started, model from native_model, project, git_branch and other v1 columns from their v2 locations or archived values, five token counters, cost_usd from the transitional cost location, provenance, source, extra) per decision D-001. The archive table is retained until Task 70 retention policy handles it (documented). SQLite and Postgres both support the view; ORM code no longer references the old model class (removed in subtask 9), so the view exists purely for external readers such as Grafana dashboards and docs/deployment/grafana.md examples. Update docs/deployment/grafana.md to state the view guarantee. Downgrade recreates the physical table from the archive. Test strategy: migration tests on both engines asserting view queryability with the exact v1 column list and types; a Grafana-style raw SQL query fixture returning identical results before and after the swap; downgrade round-trip test.

subtasks.title: logical_requests population and winning-attempt identification
subtasks.description: Maintain the logical request grouping automatically from attempt events.
subtasks.details: Extend the ingest pipeline: when an attempt event carries logical_request_id, upsert the logical_requests row (FR-TRACE-001/002): increment attempt_count on new attempts, increment fallback_count when routing.fallback_from is set, update ts_first/ts_last, store requested_model, routing policy and reason from the first attempt, and set winning_attempt_id to the attempt with outcome success and finality final (FR-TRACE-004; on multiple successes keep the last completed). Logical request summary events (event_kind logical_request) update metadata only and never contribute billable usage (FR-EVENT-004, FR-TRACE-007). Failed attempts with usage remain visible and independently billable (FR-TRACE-003/005). Session linkage uses scoped identity (provider, source, session_id) preparing FR-TRACE-011. Recompute helper for corrections (a corrected attempt updates its logical request aggregates). Test strategy: unit tests for the upsert math across multi-attempt fallback chains including out-of-order arrival (fallback attempt lands before the original), winning-attempt selection rules, and summary events adding zero usage; integration test with a three-attempt cascade fixture asserting logical_requests state and that attempt token sums are unaffected.

subtasks.title: OpenAPI v2 publication, schema endpoint, ingest docs, and performance tests
subtasks.description: Publish the v2 contract and prove the ingest performance targets.
subtasks.details: Publish GET /api/v2/schemas/usage-event serving the JSON schema from subtask 1; ensure the FastAPI OpenAPI document fully describes all v2 ingest schemas, error formats, and version negotiation notes (FR-INGEST-011/012); write docs/api/ingest-v2.md covering the envelope, event lifecycle semantics (snapshot, final, correction), idempotency guidance for exporters (replay on ambiguous transport failure is safe), batch limits, gzip, scopes, and the validate endpoint; add docs/architecture/event-model-v2.md describing the ledger, revisions, and logical requests. Performance tests (pytest markers, runnable in CI nightly and locally): 100-event batch ingest p95 under 200 ms on reference hardware (NFR-PERF-001), a 5000-event v1 compatibility batch (PRD 18.5), and a sustained-throughput smoke preparing the full 1000 events per second gate that Task 70 owns (NFR-PERF-002). Record measured numbers in the task log and docs. Test strategy: OpenAPI snapshot test pinning the v2 paths; docs build check; the two performance suites with recorded baselines; epic acceptance walkthrough of PRD Epic TOK-3 criteria before marking the parent done.

tasks.title: TOK-4: Source Registry and Scoped API Tokens
tasks.description: Represent gateways, collectors, SDKs, and importers as first-class reporting sources with health tracking; add token scopes and optional per-token source allowlists; enforce scopes across v1, v2, and WebSocket. PRD-TOK-002 Sections 12.6, 12.16; Epic TOK-4; FR-SOURCE-001..012, FR-SEC-001..011; decision D-011.
tasks.details:
Objective: separate exporter identity from machine identity and introduce least-privilege ingest tokens (Migration Phase 2).

Design: sources table (type collector|gateway|sdk|importer|manual, name, version, instance_id, optional machine link, token label, first_seen, last_seen, billing_mode per D-007); auto-registration from v2 payload source identity (D-011); source health (last successful ingest, recent error count, schema version, clock skew per R-009); v1 collector traffic attributed to a derived source so the existing machines table remains supported (FR-SOURCE-008, FR-SOURCE-003); source labels mutable without changing event identity (FR-SOURCE-010); revoked sources never delete history (FR-SOURCE-012). Token scopes: ingest:events, ingest:limits, ingest:aggregates, query:read, admin:tokens, admin:corrections (FR-INGEST-003 plus D-002); ingest-only tokens cannot access query endpoints (FR-INGEST-004); optional per-token source allowlist (FR-INGEST-020, FR-SEC-004); tokens remain hashed at rest with plaintext shown once (FR-SEC-001/002); bootstrap token stays full-admin for first-run (FR-SEC-008); auth failures reveal nothing about label existence (FR-SEC-010); last-used retained (FR-SEC-006); WebSocket authorization matches REST (NFR-SEC-008).

Acceptance criteria (PRD Epic TOK-4): source type and version stored; ingest tokens have scopes; sources have freshness and health status; existing machine tracking remains compatible; aiProviderProxy can receive an ingest-only token.

Test strategy: unit tests for scope authorization; integration tests for token scope enforcement (ingest-only denied on query, admin required for token and correction endpoints), revoked token rejection, WebSocket auth; migration tests for api_tokens scope columns; source auto-registration and health tests including clock-skew warning.

subtasks.title: sources table and auto-registration from v2 payloads
subtasks.description: Persist reporting source identity separately from machines.
subtasks.details: Alembic migration adding sources (id autoincrement, type collector|gateway|sdk|importer|manual per FR-SOURCE-002, name, version, instance_id nullable, machine nullable reference to machines.id, token_label nullable, billing_mode api_billed|subscription default api_billed per FR-COST-011, first_seen, last_seen, revoked bool default false) with unique constraint on (type, name, instance_id). Auto-registration service (services/sources.py): every v2 ingest resolves the payload source object to a sources row, creating on first sight (decision D-011) and updating last_seen and version; source identity is never conflated with machine identity (FR-SOURCE-003) but one machine may host multiple sources (FR-SOURCE-009); labels are mutable without changing event identity (FR-SOURCE-010); revoking a source stops accepting its events (when the token allowlist enforces it) but never deletes history (FR-SOURCE-012). usage_events_v2.source_id gains its FK now. Test strategy: unit tests for resolve-or-create idempotency and version bumping; migration tests both engines; integration test that two sources on one machine coexist and that revocation blocks nothing historically.

subtasks.title: Source health tracking service
subtasks.description: Freshness, error counts, schema version, and clock skew per source.
subtasks.details: Extend services/sources.py with health state per source: last_successful_ingest, recent_error_count (rolling window from ingest rejections attributed to the source), reported schema_version from the batch envelope, and clock skew computed from the delta between batch receipt time and max event timestamp (bounded future timestamps rejected per R-009 mitigation, skew beyond a warning threshold recorded as a clock_skew data quality event). Staleness derives from last_successful_ingest against a configurable per-source-type threshold (collectors 30 minutes, gateways 10 minutes defaults) and feeds both the dashboard sources page (Task 67) and the stale_source alert kind (Task 68) per FR-SOURCE-005/006/007. Health computation is query-time from stored fields, not a background job, to stay simple. Test strategy: unit tests for skew math including negative skew, staleness thresholds per type, and error-window accounting; integration test ingesting healthy then erroring batches and asserting the health transitions; data quality record emission test for skew.

subtasks.title: Token scopes model and migration
subtasks.description: Scoped API tokens with migration defaulting existing tokens to full access.
subtasks.details: Alembic migration adding scopes (JSONType list) and source_allowlist (JSONType list, nullable) to api_tokens. Scope vocabulary: ingest:events, ingest:limits, ingest:aggregates, query:read, admin:tokens, admin:corrections, admin:pricing, admin:retention (FR-INGEST-003 extended by decisions D-002 and D-015; admin scopes cover the administrative surfaces landing in Tasks 64 and 70). Existing tokens migrate to the full scope set so current collectors keep working (compatibility default documented); the env bootstrap token implicitly holds all scopes (FR-SEC-008). Token creation API accepts a scope list; hashing, single plaintext display, revocation, and last_used behavior unchanged (FR-SEC-001/002/005/006). services/tokens.py returns scope data with the auth principal. Test strategy: migration tests asserting existing tokens receive full scopes on upgrade and clean downgrade; unit tests for scope parsing and validation (unknown scope rejected at creation); creation and revocation round trips with scoped tokens.

subtasks.title: Scope enforcement across v1, v2, and WebSocket
subtasks.description: Least-privilege authorization on every endpoint class.
subtasks.details: Replace the flat require_token dependency with require_scopes(*scopes): v2 ingest endpoints require their ingest scope; all v1 and v2 query endpoints require query:read; token administration requires admin:tokens; correction ingest requires admin:corrections; pricing administration requires admin:pricing when Task 64 lands; the WebSocket stream requires query:read matching REST (NFR-SEC-008). Ingest-only tokens receive 403 on query endpoints (FR-INGEST-004, AC-012). V1 ingest endpoints accept ingest:events for compatibility so a scoped-down collector token works on the old wire. Optional per-token source allowlist enforcement: when set, v2 batches whose source identity is not listed are rejected with a structured error (FR-INGEST-020, FR-SEC-004). Authentication failures return a uniform 401 that never reveals whether a token label exists (FR-SEC-010). Test strategy: integration matrix over token scope sets x endpoint classes asserting allow and deny; WS connection denial without query:read; allowlist accept and reject cases; revoked and unknown token uniform-response test; golden v1 suite still green using a full-scope token.

subtasks.title: Sources API and token administration v2
subtasks.description: GET /api/v2/sources with health; scoped token lifecycle endpoints.
subtasks.details: Add api/v2/sources.py: GET /api/v2/sources returning registry rows with health (freshness state, last successful ingest, error count, schema version, clock skew) filterable by type and staleness (FR-SOURCE-001..006); PATCH for label and billing_mode mutation; revoke action. Extend token endpoints (api/tokens.py or api/v2/tokens.py): create with scopes and optional source allowlist, list showing scopes and last_used, revoke, plus a rotation flow (create replacement, revoke old) documented for FR-SEC-009. Administrative endpoints require admin scopes (FR-SEC-007); responses never expose token hashes (FR-PRIV-011). Provisioning an ingest-only token for aiProviderProxy becomes a one-call operation (Epic TOK-4 acceptance). Test strategy: integration tests for source listing with health fields and filters, label mutation preserving event identity, scoped token creation and the 403 behavior of a fresh ingest-only token on query endpoints, and hash non-exposure; OpenAPI snapshot updated.

subtasks.title: V1 source attribution for legacy collector traffic
subtasks.description: Derive source identity for v1 ingest so machine tracking stays compatible.
subtasks.details: V1 payloads carry machine info but no source object. Derive a deterministic source per v1 ingest: type collector, name claude-code-collector, instance_id from machine id, version from the batch machine_info collector_version, machine linked (FR-SOURCE-008 keeps the machines table fully supported). Attribute newly ingested v1 events to that source_id and backfill source_id for historical rows created by the 62.8 backfill (single UPDATE keyed on machine, batched). The existing collector_stale alert keeps functioning on machines while the new stale_source kind (Task 68) works on sources; both coexist. Machine API endpoints unchanged. Test strategy: integration test that v1 ingest auto-creates exactly one source per machine and reuses it across batches; backfill attribution test on seeded historical data; regression that /api/v1/machines output is unchanged and golden suite green.

subtasks.title: Epic tests and source-health documentation
subtasks.description: Round out coverage and write docs/architecture/source-health.md.
subtasks.details: Write docs/architecture/source-health.md: source model and identity rules, health fields and thresholds, staleness semantics per source type, clock-skew policy, token scope vocabulary with least-privilege examples (collector token: ingest:events plus ingest:limits; gateway token: ingest:events; dashboard token: query:read), source allowlists, rotation runbook, and the v1 derived-source behavior. Verify epic acceptance criteria: source type and version stored; tokens scoped; freshness and health visible via API; machine tracking compatible; an ingest-only token provisioned end to end. Extend the shared fixtures with a registered gateway source (aiProviderProxy-shaped) used by Tasks 65 and 67. Full pytest suite green with coverage threshold; docs reviewed against FR-SOURCE-001..012 and FR-SEC-001..011 with each requirement implemented or explicitly deferred. Test strategy: documentation review checklist plus the acceptance-criteria integration test added under apps/server/tests/integration/test_source_epic_acceptance.py.

tasks.title: TOK-5: Pricing Rate Cards and Cost Engine v2
tasks.description: Replace fixed token price rows with a generic rate-card system plus billable units; separate computed costs from immutable usage; add billing modes for dual cost metrics; async cost computation with auditable repricing; LiteLLM import with dry-run diff. PRD-TOK-002 Sections 12.7, 12.8; Epic TOK-5; FR-PRICE-001..022, FR-COST-001..013; decisions D-006, D-007, D-015, D-016.
tasks.details:
Objective: provider-neutral pricing that preserves historical costs exactly (Migration Phase 2 and 3).

Design: rate_cards table with provider, native_model, unit_type, effective_from, optional effective_to, currency, region, service_tier, mode realtime|batch, context_bracket, decimal unit_price, source, verified_at, priority, override flag (D-006); overlap rejection (FR-PRICE-005); unit-type vocabulary from PRD 12.7.2 (input_token, output_token, cache_read_token, cache_write_short_token, cache_write_long_token, reasoning_token, request, web_search_request, tool_call, image_input, image_output, audio_input_second, audio_output_second, video_second, storage_byte_hour, batch_input_token, batch_output_token); billable_units table for non-token counts per event (FR-DIM-007/008). computed_costs separate from usage rows: cost_status priced|unpriced|partial|estimated|error, pricing_version, currency, calculated_at, observed_cost for exporter reconciliation (FR-COST-001..006, D-016); missing pricing yields unpriced, never a silent default (FR-PRICE-002); partial names missing units (FR-COST-007). Cost computation asynchronous so ingest is never rejected by pricing failures (FR-COST-008/009, NFR-REL-007); repricing is an explicit, audited, reversible admin operation (FR-PRICE-019/020). Billing modes: every source or account carries billing_mode api_billed|subscription (FR-COST-011); rollups and APIs expose actual API spend and subscription-equivalent value separately, never merged (FR-COST-012/013, D-007). Existing pricing rows migrate losslessly into rate_cards without changing historical results (FR-PRICE-009); Anthropic cache short and long writes, OpenAI cached input and reasoning, and Z.ai cached input remain supported (FR-PRICE-010..012). Imports: curated official sources preferred, LiteLLM labeled fallback; every import produces a dry-run diff requiring explicit audited apply (FR-PRICE-014/015/021, D-015). Decimal arithmetic only (FR-PRICE-017).

Acceptance criteria (PRD Epic TOK-5): existing pricing migrates without historical change; Anthropic, OpenAI, and Z.ai rate cards supported; missing prices produce unpriced status; cost records retain pricing version and source; search and tool request charges and cached input representable; dry-run price import and diff exist.

Test strategy: unit tests for pricing selection (tier, mode, context bracket, priority, override) and decimal cost computation and billable unit aggregation; regression test that historical costs are bit-identical after adding new rates (AC-007); migration-equality test that recomputing v1 events under rate_cards equals stored v1 costs; integration tests for missing pricing, unpriced reports, import dry-run and apply auditing.

subtasks.title: rate_cards table and lossless migration of existing pricing rows
subtasks.description: The generic rate-card store replacing fixed token price rows.
subtasks.details: Alembic migration creating rate_cards (id autoincrement, provider, native_model, unit_type, effective_from date, effective_to date nullable, currency default USD, region nullable, service_tier nullable, mode realtime|batch default realtime, context_bracket nullable string like gt200k, unit_price Numeric(20,10) per-unit price stored per single unit with per-MTok conversion handled at import, source, verified_at, priority int default 0, override bool default false, created_at) with unique constraint over (provider, native_model, unit_type, effective_from, coalesced tier, mode, context_bracket, priority) and index on (provider, native_model, unit_type, effective_from). Data migration: each existing pricing row expands into five rate_cards rows (input_token, output_token, cache_read_token, cache_write_short_token, cache_write_long_token) with effective_from preserved, source copied, per-MTok prices divided by 1e6 exactly in Decimal (FR-PRICE-009). The old pricing table remains untouched and read-only until subtask 11 confirms equality, then keeps serving the v1 /api/v1/pricing endpoint via adapter. Test strategy: migration tests both engines; Decimal exactness test that expanded rows reproduce original per-MTok values with zero drift; constraint tests for duplicate rejection; downgrade drops rate_cards leaving pricing intact.

subtasks.title: billable_units table and unit-type vocabulary
subtasks.description: Non-token billable unit storage per attempt event.
subtasks.details: Define the unit-type vocabulary as a core constant module in packages/core (tokemetry_core/units.py) with the PRD 12.7.2 list: input_token, output_token, cache_read_token, cache_write_short_token, cache_write_long_token, reasoning_token, request, web_search_request, tool_call, image_input, image_output, audio_input_second, audio_output_second, video_second, storage_byte_hour, batch_input_token, batch_output_token; token unit types map one to one onto the typed counters of usage_events_v2 and are never stored in billable_units (PP-007, FR-DIM-007). Alembic migration creating billable_units (provider, event_id, unit_type, quantity Numeric(20,6)) with composite unique (provider, event_id, unit_type) and FK-style reference to usage_events_v2. Ingest wiring: v2 events may carry a billable_units map (added to the wire model as an optional bounded dict validated against the vocabulary); hosted-tool charges like web_search_request land here (FR-DIM-008); unknown counters remain in provider extension metadata (FR-EVENT-016) until promoted to vocabulary. Test strategy: unit tests for vocabulary validation and rejection of token types in the map; migration tests; ingest round-trip test storing and re-reading unit rows; revision-engine test that superseding an event replaces its unit rows atomically.

subtasks.title: Rate resolution service
subtasks.description: Effective-date, tier, mode, bracket, and priority aware price lookup with overlap rejection.
subtasks.details: Add services/pricing_v2.py: resolve(provider, native_model, unit_type, at_ts, tier, mode, context_bracket) returning the applicable rate. Selection rules: candidate rows where effective_from <= at_ts date and (effective_to null or >= date), matching unit_type and mode, tier match or null-tier fallback, bracket match or null-bracket fallback, then highest priority wins with override rows beating non-override at equal priority (FR-PRICE-004 documented precedence); dated-model to base-model fallback preserved from the current PricingTable.resolve base_model_id logic so dated Claude ids keep resolving. Overlap validation on write: two rows with identical (provider, model, unit_type, tier, mode, bracket, priority) and intersecting date ranges are rejected (FR-PRICE-005). Historical cost always uses the rate effective at event time (FR-PRICE-001); adding rows with later effective_from never changes past results (FR-PRICE-016 for automation paths). All arithmetic Decimal (FR-PRICE-017). Test strategy: exhaustive unit tests over the precedence lattice (tier and bracket fallbacks, priority, override), date-boundary tests at exact effective_from midnight UTC, overlap rejection matrix, base-id fallback parity against existing pricing/table.py tests.

subtasks.title: computed_costs table separating cost from usage facts
subtasks.description: Cost records with status, pricing version, and reconciliation fields.
subtasks.details: Alembic migration creating computed_costs (provider, event_id referencing usage_events_v2, pricing_version string, cost_status priced|unpriced|partial|estimated|error per FR-COST-006, amount Numeric(20,10) nullable, currency, billing_mode api_billed|subscription, subscription_equivalent_amount Numeric(20,10) nullable, missing_units JSONType nullable per FR-COST-007, observed_cost Numeric(20,10) nullable for exporter reconciliation per FR-COST-003 and decision D-016, calculated_at, active bool) with unique constraint on (provider, event_id, pricing_version) and a partial-unique concept for one active row per event per pricing version (FR-COST-001; enforce in service layer for SQLite compatibility). Backfill: the transitional v1 cost carried through 62.8 materializes as computed_costs rows with pricing_version v1-legacy and cost_status priced (or unpriced where cost was NULL), after which the transitional column on the ledger is dropped and the compatibility view of 62.10 re-points its cost_usd projection to the active computed cost. Observed cost never replaces authoritative cost automatically (FR-COST-004); drift between observed and computed is exposed by a query helper (FR-COST-005). Test strategy: migration and backfill equality tests (every v1 cost value reproduced exactly, NULLs become unpriced); active-row uniqueness service tests; view projection regression via the golden suite.

subtasks.title: Cost engine v2 with unpriced and partial semantics
subtasks.description: Compute cost from token counters plus billable units against rate cards.
subtasks.details: Rewrite services/cost.py as CostEngineV2: for a final attempt event, gather the applicable unit quantities (six token counters mapped to their unit types, reasoning priced only when a reasoning_token rate exists otherwise folded into output per provider strategy, plus billable_units rows), resolve each through the rate service, sum Decimal amounts quantized to 0.000001, and write a computed_costs row with pricing_version equal to a monotonically increasing pricing-state identifier (hash or counter bumped on every rate card apply, FR-PRICE-018). Missing rate for any consumed unit type yields cost_status partial with missing_units listing them; no rates at all yields unpriced with amount null (FR-PRICE-002, PP-008); resolver errors yield error status with detail in the task log, never an ingest rejection (FR-COST-008). Snapshots are not priced; only finality final attempt events receive cost records (FR-COST-001). Hosted tool and search request fees are additive to token fees (FR-PRICE-013). Test strategy: unit tests for priced, partial, unpriced, and error paths, additive unit fees, reasoning fold-in versus separate pricing, quantization, and pricing_version stamping; property test that recomputing with an unchanged pricing state is idempotent.

subtasks.title: Asynchronous cost worker and auditable repricing job
subtasks.description: Cost computation off the ingest path; explicit reversible repricing.
subtasks.details: Add a background cost worker (asyncio task started in app.py like the alert engine loop): consumes newly finalized events (queue fed by the ingest service plus a sweep for events lacking an active cost record) and writes computed_costs; ingest latency is therefore independent of pricing (FR-COST-009, NFR-PERF-005) and cost failure never rejects usage (NFR-REL-007). Repricing: an explicit administrative operation (POST /api/v2/pricing/reprice with time range and optional provider/model filters) that computes costs under the current pricing state as new computed_costs rows with the new pricing_version, flips active atomically per event, retains prior rows for reversibility (FR-PRICE-019/020), records an audit entry with actor, filters, affected counts, and pricing versions, and triggers rollup recomputation for affected days (FR-ROLLUP-009 dependency noted for Task 66). A revert endpoint re-activates a named prior pricing_version for the same range. Test strategy: worker tests for eventual cost coverage after bursts and after worker restart (sweep catch-up); repricing integration test proving old rows retained, active flips, revert restores exact prior amounts, and audit entries written; ingest-latency isolation test with a deliberately failing resolver.

subtasks.title: Provider pricing strategy plugins: anthropic, openai, zai, generic
subtasks.description: Per-provider unit semantics behind the plugin interface.
subtasks.details: Extend the PricingStrategy abstraction in packages/core (interfaces.py) to v2 semantics: a strategy declares which unit types a provider emits, how provider-reported counters map onto them, and provider-specific rules. Implement four plugins under packages/core pricing/strategies/: anthropic (port of current behavior; cache_write_short and cache_write_long distinct per FR-PRICE-010; reasoning folded into output since Anthropic does not bill it separately); openai (cached input tokens map to cache_read_token; a single cache-write concept must not be misrepresented as Anthropic TTL categories, metadata notes the semantic per FR-DIM-006; reasoning_tokens priced as output unless a separate reasoning rate exists per FR-PRICE-011; web_search_request and tool request fees additive); zai (GLM cached input pricing per FR-PRICE-012, reasoning handling per provider docs); generic (plain token-linear fallback for unregistered providers, NFR-MAIN-002). Strategies register through the existing provider registry (registry.py) so new providers are plugins without core changes (PP-011, NFR-MAIN-002, memory rule design-for-multi-provider). Test strategy: per-strategy unit tests with provider-realistic fixtures (Anthropic event with both cache TTL writes; OpenAI event with cached input plus reasoning plus web search; Z.ai event with cached input); a fake-provider test proving the abstraction (fake.py pattern); registry resolution tests.

subtasks.title: Billing modes and dual cost metrics
subtasks.description: Actual API spend versus subscription-equivalent value, never merged.
subtasks.details: Implement decision D-007 and FR-COST-011..013: billing_mode lives on sources (Task 63 schema) with api_billed and subscription values; an account-level override map in settings covers non-source-attributed usage (v1 collector events from a Max subscription machine default to subscription via config, documented for the existing deployment). Cost engine writes billing_mode onto every computed_costs row: api_billed rows populate amount (actual spend); subscription rows populate subscription_equivalent_amount at equivalent API rates with amount null (no real spend). Aggregation helpers expose actual_spend_usd and subscription_value_usd as separate Decimal sums; nothing ever adds them together (FR-COST-012). The existing dashboard plan-value multiple semantics map onto subscription_value for continuity. Rollup columns land in Task 66; this subtask delivers the computation and query helpers plus settings plumbing. Test strategy: unit tests for mode resolution precedence (source mode, account override, default), dual-metric computation on mixed fixtures (Claude subscription plus OpenAI api_billed), and the never-merged invariant (helper has no combined total); integration test that v1-ingested events on a subscription-configured machine produce subscription-valued cost rows.

subtasks.title: LiteLLM import v2 with dry-run diff and audited apply
subtasks.description: Price imports land as reviewable diffs, never silent rewrites.
subtasks.details: Rework the import pipeline: packages/core pricing/sources/litellm.py transforms the LiteLLM JSON into rate_cards rows for all three providers (extending the current anthropic-only sync), carrying source litellm and verified_at; a curated official source module (pricing/sources/curated.py) holds hand-verified provider prices with source official taking precedence by priority (FR-PRICE-021). Import flow per decision D-015: POST /api/v2/pricing/import?dry_run=true returns a structured diff (new rows, superseded rows via effective_to closure, conflicts, unchanged) without persisting; apply requires a second call referencing the dry-run digest, requires admin:pricing scope, writes an audit entry (FR-PRICE-015), and never rewrites past effective periods silently (FR-PRICE-016; changes to current prices close the old row with effective_to and open a new row effective today). The legacy /api/v1/pricing/sync-litellm endpoint keeps working by internally running dry-run plus auto-apply for v1 compatibility, labeled in audit. Test strategy: transform unit tests against a recorded LiteLLM fixture for all three providers including the cache multiplier fallbacks; diff correctness tests (idempotent re-import yields empty diff); apply audit and effective-date closure tests; digest mismatch rejection test.

subtasks.title: Pricing administration API v2 and unpriced reports
subtasks.description: Rate-card CRUD, import endpoints, and unknown-model and unpriced-event reporting.
subtasks.details: Add api/v2/pricing.py: GET /api/v2/pricing (rate cards filterable by provider, model, unit type, active-at date), POST rate card create with overlap validation, close (set effective_to), manual override creation with priority precedence (FR-PRICE-004), import dry-run and apply endpoints from subtask 9, reprice and revert endpoints from subtask 6, and GET /api/v2/pricing/reports/unpriced plus /unknown-models returning unpriced event aggregates and unknown-model observations backed by computed_costs and data_quality_events (FR-PRICE-022, US-010). All mutating endpoints require admin:pricing, are audited, and return the pricing-state version after the change. The existing /api/v1/pricing endpoints continue to serve token-shaped rows derived from rate_cards through an adapter so the current SettingsView keeps working until Task 67 replaces it. Test strategy: integration tests for CRUD with overlap rejection, override precedence effect on subsequent computation, report contents after ingesting unpriced fixtures, scope denial for non-admin tokens, and v1 pricing endpoint adapter parity against pre-migration responses.

subtasks.title: Historical-stability regression suite and pricing documentation
subtasks.description: Prove historical costs never change; document pricing-v2.
subtasks.details: Deliver the epic acceptance evidence: a regression suite that (1) ingests a fixed multi-provider fixture set, snapshots all computed costs, applies new rate cards effective today, recomputes, and asserts historical amounts are bit-identical (AC-007, FR-PRICE-001); (2) proves migration equality by recomputing a copy of the v1 dataset under rate_cards and comparing to v1-legacy costs to the last decimal (FR-PRICE-009); (3) verifies unpriced visibility end to end (unknown model event lands, report shows it, alert kind from Task 68 will consume it). Write docs/architecture/pricing-v2.md covering the rate-card model, unit vocabulary, resolution precedence, billing modes and dual metrics, import and approval flow, repricing and reversibility, and pricing_version semantics; update docs/api/query.md pricing sections. Epic acceptance walkthrough of PRD Epic TOK-5 criteria recorded in the task log before the parent closes. Test strategy: the three regression suites green on SQLite and Postgres; docs review against FR-PRICE-001..022 and FR-COST-001..013 with every requirement mapped.

tasks.title: TOK-6: aiProviderProxy Integration and Client Libraries
tasks.description: Ship the production integration contract for the aiProviderProxy (RelayPlane) exporter: mapping documentation, OpenAPI-generated Python and TypeScript clients with batching and retry wrappers, end-to-end ingest verification for Anthropic, OpenAI, and Z.ai attempt events without double counting. PRD-TOK-002 Epic TOK-6; companion PRD FR-TOK-001..030, CanonicalUsageEvent; decisions D-012, D-016.
tasks.details:
Objective: the first direct gateway exporter works end to end; this completes the usable vertical slice (Migration Phase 3, dual write with cost reconciliation).

Design: Tokemetry owns the wire contract; the proxy transforms its CanonicalUsageEvent into the v2 usage event (companion PRD FR-TOK-003). Integration documentation at docs/integrations/ai-provider-proxy.md covers field-by-field mapping (camelCase canonical to snake_case wire), event identity and deterministic fallback IDs, finality and sequence semantics for streaming snapshots, outcome vocabulary, privacy rules (no prompt, response, file path, tool argument, or code content; FR-TOK-029), version negotiation, and batching guidance (default 100 events, 256 KiB; FR-TOK-008..010). Client libraries generated from OpenAPI with thin hand-written wrappers for auth, batching, retry with exponential backoff and jitter on 429 and 5xx, pause on 401, poison-event isolation on 400 and 422 (D-012; companion FR-TOK-011..016) published under packages/clients/python and packages/clients/typescript. Exporter-observed cost is stored as reconciliation metadata and never replaces authoritative cost (FR-COST-003/004, D-016).

Acceptance criteria (PRD Epic TOK-6): integration documentation complete; TypeScript client or examples available; proxy attempt events ingest successfully; retries and fallback attempts do not double count; requested and routed model visible; content-free payload tests pass; source freshness visible.

Test strategy: compose-based integration harness with a test server and mock proxy exporter fixtures for all three providers including retries, fallbacks, and snapshot-then-final sequences; end-to-end assertions that logical request views never double count attempt usage (AC-005/006); contract conformance suite with golden payloads shared with the proxy repository; poison-event behavior verification; ingest-only token provisioning runbook validated.

subtasks.title: Integration contract document for the proxy exporter
subtasks.description: Field-level mapping and semantics contract at docs/integrations/ai-provider-proxy.md.
subtasks.details: Write docs/integrations/ai-provider-proxy.md, the shared contract for the RelayPlane proxy exporter (companion PRD section 11.13/11.14): field-by-field mapping from CanonicalUsageEvent (camelCase) to the v2 wire event (snake_case) including eventId to event_id, logicalRequestId, attemptId, the model triple, timestamps, six token counters, success/outcome/httpStatus/stopReason/serviceTier, latencyMs and timeToFirstTokenMs, streaming, toolCallCount, routing object incl accountLabel handling (exported only when enabled, never secret material per companion FR-AUTH-008), provenance official|local_estimate, and extra namespace rules (extra.usage for unknown counters per companion FR-USAGE-008 mapping into provider-namespaced extension metadata per FR-EVENT-016). Event identity rules: provider-native ids preferred, deterministic fallback ids (companion FR-USAGE-003/004), streaming snapshots same id with increasing sequence and explicit final (FR-USAGE-005/006). Error and outcome vocabulary shared table. Privacy: prohibited content list and the audit-mode expectation that the proxy can display exact payloads before enabling export (companion NFR-PRIV-005). Version negotiation: schema_version 2, server capabilities via OpenAPI, compatibility promise. Batching and retry guidance mirroring companion FR-TOK-008..016 (100 events, 256 KiB, backoff on 429/5xx, pause on 401, poison isolation on 400/422, replay on ambiguity relying on idempotency). Test strategy: documentation review against both PRDs requirement by requirement; every mapped field cross-checked against the v2 wire model of 62.1; the mapping table becomes the source for the conformance fixtures of subtask 7.

subtasks.title: TypeScript client package generated from OpenAPI
subtasks.description: packages/clients/typescript with batching and retry wrapper.
subtasks.details: Set up packages/clients/typescript: types generated from the server OpenAPI document (openapi-typescript) in a build step, plus a small hand-written wrapper class TokemetryClient (fetch-based, no runtime dependencies beyond the platform) providing bearer auth, ingestEvents with automatic batch splitting by count and bytes, retry with exponential backoff and jitter on 429 and 5xx honoring Retry-After, fail-fast pause signaling on 401, structured validation-error surfacing for 400 and 422 so callers can implement poison isolation, and a validate() helper hitting the validate endpoint (decision D-012; companion FR-TOK-011..016 client side). Strict TypeScript, ESLint per repo conventions, vitest tests, 2-space indent per code style. Package private-publishable (npm pack) so the proxy repo can consume it or vendor the generated types. Regeneration script wired into CI so schema drift fails the build. Test strategy: vitest unit tests with a mocked fetch covering batch splitting boundaries, backoff schedule determinism (injected RNG), 401 pause behavior, error-shape parsing against the 62.6 error snapshot, and gzip encoding when available; a smoke test against a live local server in the integration harness of subtask 4.

subtasks.title: Python client package generated from OpenAPI
subtasks.description: packages/clients/python for SDK exporters and importers.
subtasks.details: Set up packages/clients/python as a uv workspace member: models generated from OpenAPI (openapi-python-client or datamodel-code-generator emitting pydantic v2 models) plus a hand-written TokemetryClient wrapper (httpx-based, sync and async variants) with the same semantics as the TypeScript wrapper: auth, batch splitting, backoff on 429/5xx, pause on 401, structured error surfacing, validate helper (D-012). Full type hints, mypy strict, ruff clean, docstrings per documentation standards. This client is also the internal tool for importers and the OTLP bridge tests. Regeneration script in CI detecting drift. Test strategy: pytest unit tests mirroring the TypeScript suite (respx-mocked transport) plus a round-trip integration test in the subtask-4 harness ingesting a fixture batch and reading it back through the query API; packaging test (uv build) and import-from-wheel smoke.

subtasks.title: Integration test harness with mock proxy exporter fixtures
subtasks.description: Compose-based test rig replaying realistic proxy exports for all three providers.
subtasks.details: Build the integration rig under apps/server/tests/integration/proxy_harness/: a fixture library of realistic proxy export batches for anthropic (cache read plus short and long writes, streaming snapshot sequence ending in final), openai (cached input, reasoning tokens, web_search_request billable unit, service tier), and zai (GLM ids, cached input), each with logical requests spanning multiple attempts (retry after 429, fallback to a second provider with fallback_from and fallback_trigger set), client-cancelled attempts with partial usage, and failed attempts with zero tokens (FR-EVENT-024). A replay driver posts these through the real HTTP surface using the Python client with an ingest-only token. Optionally a docker compose profile (deploy/compose.test.yml) running server plus Postgres for the Postgres leg; SQLite leg runs in-process. The fixtures are the shared truth also exported for the proxy repository (subtask 7). Test strategy: the harness itself ships with assertions that every fixture batch ingests with expected accepted/updated counts and that re-running the driver is a pure no-op (replay safety, AC-003); fixture JSON validated against the published schema from 62.12.

subtasks.title: End-to-end verification: three providers, no double counting, model visibility
subtasks.description: Prove the epic acceptance criteria on the full stack.
subtasks.details: End-to-end tests driving harness fixtures through server, query API, and rollups: (1) per-provider event landing tests asserting usage, cache and reasoning dimensions, billable units, and computed costs appear correctly for anthropic, openai, and zai (AC-002); (2) fallback chains: a logical request with three attempts counts every attempt independently in attempt views and exactly once in usage totals, logical request views show attempt_count, fallback_count, and winning attempt without adding token or cost totals to attempt totals (FR-TRACE-007, AC-005/006); (3) requested versus routed versus native model visible and distinct in attempt responses (US-005); (4) streaming snapshot sequences resolve to exactly one final usage record (AC-004); (5) duplicate batch replay does not inflate totals (AC-003). Where Task 66 endpoints are not yet merged, assertions run at the service layer with a follow-up marker to extend to HTTP once 66 lands (ordering note: this epic may run in parallel with 66). Test strategy: this subtask is the test suite; completion requires all five scenario groups green on SQLite and Postgres.

subtasks.title: Provisioning runbook, source freshness, and cost reconciliation drift
subtasks.description: Operational integration: token setup, exporter health, observed-versus-computed cost.
subtasks.details: Write the operator runbook section of docs/integrations/ai-provider-proxy.md: provisioning an ingest-only token (scopes ingest:events plus ingest:limits, optional source allowlist binding to the proxy source identity), configuring the proxy exporter (baseUrl, token reference not embedded, queue path per companion config), verifying connectivity with relayplane tokemetry test-connection against the validate endpoint, and monitoring exporter health through GET /api/v2/sources freshness. Implement the reconciliation drift query: observed_cost from proxy events versus Tokemetry computed cost, aggregated per provider and day with percentage drift, exposed through the costs query surface (FR-COST-005, US-013; UI panel lands in Task 67). Verify source freshness reflects a silent exporter within threshold (Epic acceptance: source freshness visible). Test strategy: integration test provisioning a scoped token end to end and asserting query denial; drift computation unit tests with agreeing and drifting fixtures; freshness transition test with clock injection; runbook walked through manually against the harness and corrections folded back into the doc.

subtasks.title: Contract conformance suite and content-free payload verification
subtasks.description: Golden payloads shared with the proxy repo; poison and privacy behavior locked.
subtasks.details: Produce the conformance artifacts the proxy team builds against: a versioned directory of golden request and response pairs (valid batches for all three providers with exact expected ingest responses; invalid batches with exact expected structured errors) exported from the harness fixtures, published under docs/integrations/conformance/ and mirrored as JSON fixtures the proxy repository can vendor (companion FR-TOK-030 snapshot testing hook). Content-free verification: fuzzed payloads embedding prompts, file paths, tool arguments, and code snippets in every extension point are rejected or stripped per policy with the exact error surfaced (AC-011; companion FR-TOK-029); poison-event behavior verified: a batch with one invalid event returns an error identifying the index so recursive splitting terminates (companion FR-TOK-015 client contract). Version the conformance set (v2.0) and document the compatibility promise for future schema evolution (PP-010). Test strategy: conformance suite runs in CI comparing live server responses to golden files; privacy fuzz corpus green; a change to any v2 schema that alters a golden response fails CI until the conformance set is versioned forward deliberately.

tasks.title: TOK-7: Query API v2 and Rollups v2
tasks.description: Provider-neutral query endpoints for usage, costs, requests, attempts, sessions, limits, data quality, pricing, and rollups; new rollup grain with source, environment, billing mode, reasoning and cache dimensions; stable pagination; CSV export; Grafana views; v1 query parity. PRD-TOK-002 Sections 12.12, 12.13; Epic TOK-7; FR-QUERY-001..014, FR-ROLLUP-001..012.
tasks.details:
Objective: provider-neutral analytics without double counting (Migration Phase 4).

Design: daily_rollups evolves to grain (day, provider, native_model, machine, project, source, environment, billing_mode, provenance) adding reasoning and cache dimensions and cost split by cost_status (FR-ROLLUP-004..007); rollups derive exclusively from final attempt events, never snapshots after a final exists and never logical summaries (FR-ROLLUP-001..003); refresh is incremental and idempotent with correction-triggered affected-period recomputation (FR-ROLLUP-008/009). Query framework: keyset pagination (FR-QUERY-003), explicit sort (FR-QUERY-004), selectable aggregation grain where safe (FR-QUERY-005), bounded time ranges for raw high-cardinality queries (NFR-PERF-004), data-quality warnings in responses (FR-QUERY-010), unknown provider and model filters (FR-QUERY-011). Endpoints: GET /api/v2/usage, /api/v2/costs (dual metrics, cost status, pricing version per FR-QUERY-006), /api/v2/requests, /api/v2/attempts, /api/v2/sessions (scoped identity per FR-TRACE-011, winning attempt, fallback filters), /api/v2/limits, /api/v2/data-quality, /api/v2/pricing, /api/v2/rollups. CSV export (FR-QUERY-009) and Grafana-compatible database views (FR-QUERY-014). Existing v1 query endpoints continue to work against the v2 ledger through the service layer (FR-QUERY-012).

Acceptance criteria (PRD Epic TOK-7): v2 query endpoints exist; attempt and logical request semantics prevent double counting; cache and reasoning usage queryable; cost status visible; rollups include provider-neutral dimensions; v1 query endpoints remain functional.

Test strategy: unit tests for the query framework and rollup aggregation; integration tests for rollup refresh, correction recomputation, and double-counting prevention with multi-attempt fixtures; v1 parity regression suite comparing pre-migration and post-migration v1 responses; performance benchmarks for 30-day aggregated views p95 under 500 ms and high-cardinality project and session dimensions (NFR-PERF-003, FR-ROLLUP-012).

subtasks.title: daily_rollups v2 grain migration
subtasks.description: Extend the rollup table to the provider-neutral grain with cost statuses.
subtasks.details: Alembic migration evolving daily_rollups: extend the grain unique constraint to (day, provider, native_model, machine, project, source, environment, billing_mode, provenance) with empty-string sentinels for absent dimensions matching the existing convention (FR-ROLLUP-004/005); add reasoning_tokens column alongside the existing five token counters (FR-ROLLUP-006 cache dimensions already present as separate counters); replace the single cost_usd with cost columns by status: cost_priced_usd, cost_partial_usd, cost_estimated_usd, unpriced_event_count, plus subscription_value_usd for the dual metric (FR-ROLLUP-007, FR-COST-012); data migration maps existing rows onto the new grain with source and environment sentinels, billing_mode from the deployment default, and cost_usd into cost_priced_usd. Grafana view compatibility: create or update a stable rollup view so existing external dashboards keep a stable column set (FR-ROLLUP-011); document the migration plan for view consumers. Test strategy: migration upgrade and downgrade tests both engines; grain uniqueness tests; data-migration equality checks (sum of new cost columns equals old cost_usd per day); view stability query fixture.

subtasks.title: Rollup service v2: final attempts only, incremental, correction-aware
subtasks.description: Recompute rollups from the v2 ledger without double counting.
subtasks.details: Rewrite services/rollups.py against usage_events_v2 joined with computed_costs: aggregation selects only event_kind attempt with finality final (FR-ROLLUP-001), never superseded snapshots (revision engine guarantees single active row, FR-ROLLUP-002) and never logical_request summaries (FR-ROLLUP-003); group by the full v2 grain with project_group folding preserved; cost columns split by cost_status and billing_mode from the active computed_costs row; provenance preserved. Refresh remains whole-affected-day recomputation triggered by ingest (matching current architecture) but now also triggered by corrections and repricing for their affected periods (FR-ROLLUP-009 consuming the Task 64 repricing hook); refresh is idempotent (FR-ROLLUP-008) and incremental per day (NFR-PERF-006). rebuild_all_rollups admin path updated. Test strategy: unit tests for grain aggregation with mixed providers, statuses, and billing modes; double-counting prevention tests (snapshot-then-final, multi-attempt logical requests, correction flow recomputing exactly the affected days); idempotency test (double refresh identical); parity test that a pure-v1-shaped dataset produces rollups summing identically to the pre-migration algorithm.

subtasks.title: Query framework: pagination, sorting, grain, bounds, warnings
subtasks.description: Shared plumbing for all v2 query endpoints.
subtasks.details: Add services/query_framework.py plus api/v2 shared dependencies: keyset pagination with opaque cursors stable under concurrent inserts (FR-QUERY-003), explicit sort parameter with documented default per resource (FR-QUERY-004), selectable aggregation grain (day, week, month) where safe (FR-QUERY-005), enforced bounded time ranges for raw event-level queries with a configurable maximum span (NFR-PERF-004), uniform filter parsing for provider, model, source, machine, project, session, agent, environment, and outcome (FR-QUERY-002) including unknown-provider and unknown-model pseudo-filters (FR-QUERY-011), and a data-quality warning envelope attached to responses when the queried range contains unpriced events, unknown models, or stale sources (FR-QUERY-010). Response models in api/v2/schemas with extra=forbid. Test strategy: unit tests for cursor stability (insert between pages, no skips or duplicates), sort and grain validation, range-bound enforcement, filter parsing matrix, and warning attachment logic with seeded data-quality conditions.

subtasks.title: Usage and cost query endpoints
subtasks.description: GET /api/v2/usage and /api/v2/costs with dual metrics.
subtasks.details: Add api/v2/usage.py and api/v2/costs.py.
