GET /api/v2/usage: time-ranged, grouped by any grain dimension (day, provider, model, machine, project, source, environment, session), served from daily_rollups for rollup grains and from the ledger for session grouping mirroring the current v1 split; returns all six token counters plus attempt counts, distinguishing attempts from logical requests explicitly in the response envelope (FR-QUERY-007/008). GET /api/v2/costs: actual_spend_usd and subscription_value_usd as separate series (FR-COST-012, decision D-007), broken down by cost_status with unpriced_event_count, and each row carrying pricing_version where a single version applies or a mixed marker (FR-QUERY-006); reconciliation drift sub-resource (observed versus computed) from Task 65.6 exposed as /api/v2/costs/reconciliation. Both use the query framework: filters, pagination, sorting, warnings; scope query:read. Test strategy: integration tests over multi-provider seeded data for every group-by, dual-metric separation assertions (no endpoint returns a merged total), cost-status breakdown and pricing-version reporting, filter and warning behavior; contract snapshot into OpenAPI.

subtasks.title: Request, attempt, and session query endpoints
subtasks.description: GET /api/v2/requests, /api/v2/attempts, /api/v2/sessions.
subtasks.details: Add api/v2/requests.py and api/v2/sessions.py. GET /api/v2/attempts: event-level listing with all lifecycle fields, bounded range, filterable by outcome, provider, model, source, logical_request_id (FR-QUERY-002); the raw high-cardinality surface requiring bounded ranges. GET /api/v2/requests: logical_requests joined with attempt aggregates (attempt_count, fallback_count, winning attempt, latency span, token and cost totals computed from attempts only, FR-TRACE-007/012), filterable by routing policy and fallback trigger (FR-TRACE-006). GET /api/v2/requests/{provider}/{logical_request_id}: full drilldown with ordered attempts for the fallback-chain UI. GET /api/v2/sessions and /api/v2/sessions/{scoped_id}: session rollups from scoped identity (provider, source, session_id per FR-TRACE-011) with attempt count, fallback count, latency, tokens, cost, preserving current Claude Code session aggregation semantics (FR-TRACE-010; existing v1 sessions endpoints keep serving the old shape). Test strategy: integration tests for drilldown ordering, winning-attempt marking, per-filter coverage, scoped-identity collision test (same session_id from two sources stays separate), and v1 session endpoint parity via golden suite.

subtasks.title: Limits, data-quality, pricing, and rollup query endpoints
subtasks.description: Remaining v2 read resources.
subtasks.details: Add the remaining read endpoints (api/v2/): GET /api/v2/limits returning current and historical limit snapshots across the v2 dimensions with official versus estimated provenance (FR-LIMIT-004; window labels resolved through the registry once Task 69 lands, opaque kinds until then); GET /api/v2/data-quality listing data_quality_events filterable by kind, subject, source, resolved state (FR-QUERY resources; feeds the Task 67 page); GET /api/v2/pricing read-only rate-card listing for display (admin mutation lives in Task 64); GET /api/v2/rollups exposing daily_rollups rows directly for external tooling with the stable column contract. All through the query framework with scope query:read. /api/v2/sources ships in Task 63. Test strategy: integration tests per endpoint covering filters, pagination, provenance labeling, and the rollup column contract snapshot; scope denial tests; OpenAPI snapshot.

subtasks.title: CSV export and Grafana-compatible views
subtasks.description: Tabular export and stable SQL views for external dashboards.
subtasks.details: CSV export: a format=csv parameter (or Accept negotiation) on /api/v2/usage, /api/v2/costs, /api/v2/attempts, and /api/v2/rollups streaming RFC 4180 output with a stable header row (FR-QUERY-009); size-bounded by the same range limits. Grafana views: versioned SQL views (grafana_daily_usage_v2, grafana_costs_v2, grafana_limits_v2, plus the preserved v1 usage_events compatibility view from 62.10) using only standard SQL features available on SQLite and Postgres (G-018, FR-QUERY-014); shipped as an Alembic migration; documented in docs/deployment/grafana.md with example panel queries for multi-provider usage, dual cost metrics, and fallback rate. View schema changes require a documented migration plan (FR-ROLLUP-011). Test strategy: CSV round-trip tests (parse the stream back and compare to JSON responses), header stability snapshot, view query fixtures on both engines returning identical data to the API, docs examples executed verbatim in a test.

subtasks.title: Query and rollup performance benchmarks
subtasks.description: Measured evidence for the performance NFRs on reference hardware.
subtasks.details: Build a benchmark suite (pytest-benchmark or a scripted harness under scripts/) with a documented reference dataset generator: 90 days of synthetic multi-provider data at realistic volume (configurable, default 1M attempt events, high-cardinality sessions and projects per PRD 18.5). Measure: 30-day aggregated usage and cost queries p95 under 500 ms (NFR-PERF-003); attempt listing with bounded ranges; rollup full-day refresh and correction-triggered recomputation duration (FR-ROLLUP-012); high-cardinality group-by behavior. Record results in docs/architecture/performance.md with hardware notes and store the harness so Task 70 reuses it for the sustained-ingest gate. Identify and fix any missing indexes surfaced by the benchmarks (EXPLAIN output reviewed on Postgres). Test strategy: the benchmark harness runs in CI nightly profile with regression thresholds (soft warning at 20 percent regression); the measured numbers and index changes are recorded in the task log and the performance doc.

subtasks.title: V1 query parity regression suite and epic documentation
subtasks.description: Prove v1 endpoints unchanged; document query-v2.
subtasks.details: Extend the golden suite: after the full v2 query stack is live, replay the 60.4 fixtures and assert every /api/v1 query response byte-identical (FR-QUERY-012, AC-001 query side); add differential tests that the same underlying data queried through v1 and v2 endpoints agrees on totals (accounting consistency, no double counting across API generations, FR-QUERY-008). Write docs/api/query-v2.md documenting every v2 resource, filters, pagination cursors, sort defaults, grain rules, warning envelope, CSV export, and the attempt-versus-logical-request semantics with worked fallback examples; update docs/deployment/api-clients.md for the v2 clients. Epic acceptance walkthrough of PRD Epic TOK-7 criteria in the task log. Test strategy: golden and differential suites green on both engines; docs review mapping FR-QUERY-001..014 and FR-ROLLUP-001..012 to implementations; coverage threshold held.

tasks.title: TOK-8: Dashboard Generalization
tasks.description: Replace Claude-only dashboard assumptions with provider-neutral views: global provider and model filters, dual cost metrics, request and fallback-chain visualization, source health, data quality, and pricing administration pages, while preserving the limit-centric Claude views. PRD-TOK-002 Section 12.14; Epic TOK-8; FR-UI-001..015; decisions D-007, D-017.
tasks.details:
Objective: provider-neutral UI on top of the v2 query APIs (Migration Phase 5). Dashboard work never combines with core ingest changes (PRD Section 23 rule 11).

Design: global provider and model filter store applied across views (FR-UI-002); v2 typed API client layer generated from OpenAPI; overview covers all providers (FR-UI-001); token and cost charts distinguish cache, reasoning, input, and output (FR-UI-003); cost views expose actual API spend and subscription-equivalent value separately (D-007, FR-COST-012) plus cost status and reconciliation drift (FR-UI-008/013); request detail shows the logical request with its attempts and visualizes fallback chains (FR-UI-004/005); latency and time-to-first-token chartable (FR-UI-006); failure rate by provider and model chartable (FR-UI-007); sources page shows freshness and schema-version drift (FR-UI-009); data quality page shows unknown models and unpriced events; pricing administration page manages rate cards with dry-run diff review and apply; labels distinguish zero, unavailable, and unsupported values (FR-DIM-010) and official versus estimated (FR-UI-012); limits pages read window labels from the provider window registry instead of hardcoded WINDOW_LABELS while preserving the existing Claude block and prediction views (FR-UI-010/014); no routes removed (D-017); components must not hardcode provider sets (NFR-MAIN-006).

Acceptance criteria (PRD Epic TOK-8): global provider and model filters exist; requests and fallback chains visible; source health and data quality visible; unknown and unpriced usage visible; cache, reasoning, input, and output distinguishable; existing Claude limit views remain useful.

Test strategy: vitest unit tests for the filter store, client layer, formatting, and label logic; component tests for the fallback-chain visualization and dual-metric cost views; manual end-to-end verification against a server seeded with three-provider fixtures; regression check that all pre-existing routes still render.

subtasks.title: Global provider and model filter store with v2 API client layer
subtasks.description: Foundation: typed v2 client and cross-view filters.
subtasks.details: In apps/dashboard: generate v2 API types from the server OpenAPI document into src/api/types-v2.ts (build script, drift-checked in CI) and extend src/api/client.ts with the v2 endpoints (usage, costs, requests, attempts, sessions, sources, limits, data-quality, pricing, rollups, providers, models). Add a Pinia-style or composable filter store (src/composables/useGlobalFilters.ts) holding provider and model selections sourced from GET /api/v2/providers and /api/v2/models, persisted in the URL query and localStorage, applied by every view (FR-UI-002); components must never hardcode provider sets (NFR-MAIN-006) — provider lists, colors, and labels derive from registry data through a palette assignment helper extending src/lib/palette.ts. FilterBar component gains the provider and model selectors. Test strategy: vitest tests for the store (persistence, URL sync, multi-select), client layer request shaping, and palette assignment stability; type-generation drift test in CI.

subtasks.title: Overview generalization
subtasks.description: NowView covers all providers with official-versus-estimated and unavailability labeling.
subtasks.details: Generalize NowView (src/views/NowView.vue): the live snapshot aggregates all providers honoring global filters (FR-UI-001); limit gauges render per provider from window-registry metadata instead of assuming the four Anthropic windows (labels resolved from the registry endpoint; until Task 69 data exists, Anthropic windows continue rendering identically via registry seed); burn rate and exhaustion prediction sections scope to providers that expose subscription windows; every metric tile distinguishes official versus estimated provenance (FR-UI-012) and zero versus unavailable versus unsupported values (FR-DIM-010) using a shared LabeledValue component; the plan-value multiple tile becomes the subscription-value metric consistent with dual metrics. WebSocket live refresh unchanged. Test strategy: vitest component tests with three-provider fixture payloads asserting gauge sets, label states, and filter application; visual verification against the seeded dev server; regression check that a pure-Anthropic deployment renders equivalently to today.

subtasks.title: Usage views with cache and reasoning composition
subtasks.description: TrendsView and BreakdownsView on v2 usage data.
subtasks.details: Move TrendsView and BreakdownsView to /api/v2/usage: stacked composition charts distinguish uncached input, cache reads, cache writes (short and long), output, and reasoning (FR-UI-003, FR-DIM-001/005); group-by dimension pickers extend to source and environment; model labels come from the registry (native id shown with normalized display name per FR-MODEL-010) replacing the Claude-specific modelLabel parser while keeping Claude ids rendering as today; the cache-savings tile derives from rate-card prices via the costs endpoint instead of client-side pricing math; provider-specific copy (Claude cache guidance) becomes conditional on provider context. Heatmap and punch card gain provider filtering. Test strategy: vitest tests for composition math, label resolution fallbacks (unknown model shows native id plus unknown badge), and group-by wiring; fixture-driven rendering tests for mixed-provider data; manual dev-server verification.

subtasks.title: Cost views with dual metrics and reconciliation
subtasks.description: Actual spend versus subscription value, cost status, drift panel.
subtasks.details: Build the cost experience on /api/v2/costs: charts and tables always present actual API spend and subscription-equivalent value as two visually distinct series, never a merged total (FR-COST-012, decision D-007); cost rows show cost_status with unpriced and partial called out (FR-UI-008) linking to the data quality page; a reconciliation panel visualizes observed-versus-computed drift per provider and day from /api/v2/costs/reconciliation (FR-UI-013); pricing-version annotations appear on repriced ranges. Extend the existing /cost route content (ReportView cost sections and any cost tiles elsewhere) to the same dual-metric presentation for consistency. Follow the dataviz conventions already used by the ECharts wrappers. Test strategy: vitest tests asserting the two metrics render as separate series with correct labels for mixed fixtures, unpriced badge behavior, and drift-panel math; snapshot test that no component sums the two metrics; manual verification with subscription-Claude plus api-billed-OpenAI seed data.

subtasks.title: Requests and attempts pages with fallback-chain visualization
subtasks.description: New RequestsView: drilldown, chains, latency, failure rates.
subtasks.details: Add RequestsView (route /requests) backed by /api/v2/requests and /api/v2/attempts: a filterable logical-request table (provider, model, outcome, routing policy, fallback presence) showing attempt_count, fallback_count, winning attempt, latency, tokens, cost; row expansion renders the fallback chain as an ordered attempt timeline (requested to routed model per attempt, outcome, fallback trigger, latency, tokens) making retries and provider failover visually explicit (FR-UI-004/005); an attempts tab lists raw attempts with bounded ranges. Charts: latency and time-to-first-token distributions (FR-UI-006) and failure rate by provider and model (FR-UI-007) from attempt aggregates. Register the route and navigation entry. Test strategy: vitest component tests for the chain renderer with single-attempt, retry, and cross-provider fallback fixtures including out-of-order timestamps; filter wiring tests; manual verification against harness-seeded data from Task 65.

subtasks.title: Sessions and agents generalization
subtasks.description: SessionsView on scoped v2 identity with agent linkage.
subtasks.details: Move SessionsView to /api/v2/sessions: session list and drilldown keyed by scoped identity (provider plus source plus session_id) so sessions from different sources never collide (FR-TRACE-011) while existing Claude Code sessions render exactly as today (FR-TRACE-010); session detail gains attempt and fallback counts, latency, and cost columns from the v2 rollup (FR-TRACE-012) and shows agent_id linkage where present with parent-child grouping when agents report hierarchies (FR-TRACE-009 display side); efficiency stats (cache ratios) stay provider-aware. Test strategy: vitest tests for scoped-identity rendering (two sources with the same session_id displayed separately), agent grouping, and Claude session parity against fixtures replicating current production shapes; golden-route check that the v1-era session URLs still resolve (decision D-017).

subtasks.title: Sources and machines page with health
subtasks.description: Extend MachinesView into fleet plus exporter health.
subtasks.details: Extend MachinesView (or add a SourcesView tab at /machines) backed by /api/v2/sources: table of all reporting sources with type, name, version, instance, linked machine, billing mode, first and last seen, freshness state, last successful ingest, recent error count, reported schema version, and clock skew; stale sources visually flagged (FR-SOURCE-006, FR-UI-009); schema-version drift against the server-supported version highlighted; source label and billing-mode editing through the PATCH endpoint with safe-action confirmation; machine fleet view preserved unchanged for v1 continuity. Test strategy: vitest tests for staleness and drift flag logic, edit flow with optimistic update and rollback, and coexistence of machine and source listings; manual check that a silent mock exporter turns stale within threshold using clock-injected fixtures.

subtasks.title: Data quality page and pricing administration page
subtasks.description: Unknowns, unpriced, conflicts; rate-card management with diff review.
subtasks.details: Add DataQualityView (route /data-quality) on /api/v2/data-quality: filterable feed of unknown providers, unknown models, unpriced usage, sequence conflicts, schema drift, limit-source failures, and clock skew with resolve marking and deep links (unknown model links to pricing admin, unpriced to the affected cost range) satisfying FR-UI-008 and US-010/012. Rework the SettingsView pricing section into a PricingAdminView (route /pricing-admin): rate-card table with effective ranges, tier, mode, bracket, priority, source, and verification date; manual rate creation and closure; import flow rendering the dry-run diff (new, superseded, conflicting rows) with an explicit apply step per decision D-015; repricing launcher with range selection and revert list; every action confirms and displays the audit outcome. Admin actions are gated on an admin-scoped token in the client settings. Test strategy: vitest tests for feed filtering and deep links, diff rendering states, apply-confirmation flow, and admin-gate behavior; end-to-end manual pass creating a rate, importing with diff, and repricing against the dev server.

subtasks.title: Limits pages on the window registry, navigation, and epic verification
subtasks.description: Registry-driven limit labels; preserved Claude views; nav restructure; epic acceptance.
subtasks.details: Replace hardcoded window handling: format.ts WINDOW_LABELS and AlertsView window pickers read from the registry endpoint (window kinds, labels, period semantics per FR-LIMIT-012) so provider windows appear without dashboard code changes (FR-UI-010); BlocksView and prediction logic key off registry period metadata while preserving the existing five-hour block and weekly-cap experience for Anthropic (FR-UI-014); LimitsView sections render per provider for every provider with limit data. Navigation restructure: group views into Overview, Usage, Costs, Requests, Sessions, Limits, Fleet (sources and machines), Quality (data quality and pricing admin), Alerts, Settings; all pre-existing routes keep working (decision D-017, FR-UI-015 deferred). Epic verification: walk PRD Epic TOK-8 acceptance criteria and AC-008/009/010/019/020 against the seeded dev stack and record evidence in the task log; full vitest suite green. Test strategy: vitest tests for registry-driven label resolution with unknown-window fallback to raw kind; route regression test enumerating all v1-era paths; manual multi-provider walkthrough with screenshots attached to the PR.

tasks.title: TOK-9: Alerts and Data Quality
tasks.description: Operational alerts for reporting and accounting failures: stale sources, unpriced events, unknown models, exporter schema drift, failure rate, latency, and fallback rate, all filterable by provider, model, source, project, and environment, all content-free. PRD-TOK-002 Section 12.15; Epic TOK-9; FR-ALERT-001..010.
tasks.details:
Objective: data quality and exporter health become alertable operational signals (PP-012).

Design: extend the existing rule engine (services/alerting/) with new rule kinds: stale_source (built on source health last-ingest timestamps, replacing machine-only collector_stale semantics while keeping that rule working), unpriced_events (computed_costs cost_status unpriced over a window), unknown_model (v2 data quality events), schema_drift (exporter schema version vs supported versions), failure_rate, latency_p95, and fallback_rate (from attempt outcomes, latency, and routing metadata). All rules gain optional provider, model, source, project, and environment filters (FR-ALERT-002). Existing spend and limit alerts continue to work unchanged (FR-ALERT-001); cooldown and quiet-hours behavior preserved (FR-ALERT-009); alert context stays content-free (FR-ALERT-010); delivery channels ntfy, Telegram, and SMTP unchanged.

Acceptance criteria (PRD Epic TOK-9): stale source alerts work; unknown model and unpriced usage alerts work; failure, latency, and fallback-rate alerts work; alert context content-free; existing delivery channels supported.

Test strategy: unit tests per evaluator with synthetic v2 fixtures covering warning and critical thresholds, filters, cooldown, and quiet hours; integration test firing each new kind end to end into alert_events; regression tests that the four seeded default rules and existing channels behave identically; content-free context assertion tests.

subtasks.title: Alert rule dimension filters
subtasks.description: Provider, model, source, project, and environment filters on every rule.
subtasks.details: Extend the alert rule model and engine (services/alerting/): AlertRule.config gains an optional filters object (provider, model, source, project, environment lists) applied by every evaluator when scoping its query window (FR-ALERT-002); rule CRUD API and validation updated; existing rules migrate with empty filters (behavior unchanged, FR-ALERT-001). Evaluators receive a shared filtered-query helper so filter semantics are uniform. Alert event context records the matched filter scope, staying content-free (FR-ALERT-010). Cooldown, dual thresholds, quiet hours, and the firing-state machine are untouched (FR-ALERT-009). Test strategy: unit tests per evaluator with filters applied and empty; migration test for existing rules; API validation tests for unknown filter keys; regression run of the full existing alert test suite.

subtasks.title: Stale-source alert kind
subtasks.description: Alert when a reporting source stops ingesting.
subtasks.details: Add evaluator stale_source: fires when a non-revoked source has no successful ingest for longer than warn or crit minutes (thresholds per rule, defaults from source-type thresholds in Task 63.2), one alert per source with per-source firing state so multiple stale sources notify independently (FR-ALERT-003, FR-SOURCE-007, AC-010); context includes source identity, last successful ingest, and staleness duration; respects the new dimension filters (alert only on selected source types or names). The existing collector_stale machine-based rule keeps working unchanged; seed no new default rules (user opt-in). Test strategy: unit tests with clock injection for warn and crit transitions, per-source state isolation, revoked-source exclusion, and resolve-on-recovery; integration test through the engine loop into alert_events with an ntfy stub.

subtasks.title: Unpriced-event and unknown-model alert kinds
subtasks.description: Accounting-gap alerts on v2 data.
subtasks.details: Add evaluator unpriced_events: fires when the count (or cost-bearing share) of computed_costs rows with status unpriced or partial within the evaluation window crosses thresholds (FR-ALERT-004), context listing the top offending (provider, model) pairs. Rework the existing unknown_model evaluator onto v2 sources: unknown-model observations from data_quality_events and registry lifecycle unknown instead of the NULL-cost heuristic (FR-ALERT-005), preserving the existing rule kind name so configured rules keep working. Both respect dimension filters, and both link into the data-quality records they summarize. Test strategy: unit tests with seeded unpriced and partial cost fixtures across providers, threshold and filter behavior, top-offender ordering; migration-behavior test that a pre-existing unknown_model rule fires equivalently on the reworked source; content-free context assertions.

subtasks.title: Failure-rate, latency, and fallback-rate alert kinds
subtasks.description: Operational reliability alerts from attempt outcomes and routing.
subtasks.details: Add three evaluators over final attempt events in a sliding window: failure_rate (failed outcome share versus threshold, minimum sample size guard to avoid firing on tiny volumes), latency_p95 (p95 of latency_ms versus threshold milliseconds), and fallback_rate (share of logical requests with fallback_count > 0 versus threshold) per FR-ALERT-007/008; each scoped by the dimension filters so per-provider or per-model reliability rules are expressible (US-006 operational side); context carries the measured value, sample size, and scope. Window length and minimum samples configurable per rule via config. Test strategy: unit tests for each metric computation including empty and below-minimum windows, filter scoping, warn and crit transitions, and resolve behavior; property test that fallback_rate uses logical requests not attempts as denominator (no double counting); integration smoke through the engine.

subtasks.title: Exporter schema-drift alert kind
subtasks.description: Alert when a source reports an unexpected schema version or malformed batches.
subtasks.details: Add evaluator schema_drift: fires when a source reports a batch schema_version outside the server-supported set or when a source accumulates validation rejections above threshold within the window (FR-ALERT-006, US-012), using source health data from Task 63.2 and schema_drift data quality events from 61.4; context includes source identity, reported versus supported versions, and rejection counts. This catches a proxy or collector upgraded ahead of the server (or lagging behind a deprecation) before data silently degrades. Test strategy: unit tests for version-set comparison, rejection-threshold behavior, and per-source state; integration test simulating a source posting schema_version 3 batches and asserting one alert with correct context; resolve test after the source returns to a supported version.

subtasks.title: Alert epic verification, channel regression, and documentation
subtasks.description: Prove all new kinds end to end and update alerting docs.
subtasks.details: End-to-end verification: each new alert kind (stale_source, unpriced_events, reworked unknown_model, failure_rate, latency_p95, fallback_rate, schema_drift) fired through the real engine loop into alert_events and delivered through stubbed ntfy, Telegram, and SMTP notifiers proving channel compatibility (FR-ALERT-001 for existing channels); content-free context asserted across every kind by a shared prohibited-content checker (FR-ALERT-010); AlertsView rule editor supports the new kinds and dimension filters (small UI increment coordinated with Task 67 conventions); quiet hours and cooldown verified on one new kind as representative. Update docs/alerting.md with every kind, its thresholds, filters, defaults, and example rules for the three-provider deployment. Epic acceptance walkthrough of PRD Epic TOK-9 in the task log. Test strategy: the integration matrix above plus vitest coverage for the rule-editor additions; docs review against FR-ALERT-001..010; full suite green with coverage held.

tasks.title: TOK-12: Multi-Provider Limit Sources
tasks.description: Provider-neutral limit tracking: window registry with labels and period semantics, limit snapshot dimensions for account, organization, and source, new OpenAI/Codex and Z.ai coding-plan limit sources in the collector, gateway-observed rate-limit snapshots, and confidence-labeled forecasting. PRD-TOK-002 Section 12.9; Epic TOK-12; FR-LIMIT-001..013; decision D-008.
tasks.details:
Objective: subscription and rate-limit visibility for Anthropic, OpenAI/Codex, and Z.ai on one neutral schema (new epic added by decision D-008).

Design: provider window registry describing window kinds, display labels, and period semantics per provider (FR-LIMIT-012), exposed through the providers API so dashboards and alerts stop hardcoding five_hour and seven_day labels; window kinds remain provider-defined opaque identifiers in storage (FR-LIMIT-001). limit_snapshots gains account, organization, and source dimensions plus limit_amount, remaining, and unit columns (FR-LIMIT-002/003); multiple account or subscription streams are never merged without an explicit grouping rule (FR-LIMIT-005); official versus estimated provenance remains distinguishable (FR-LIMIT-004). New collector limit sources following the AnthropicOAuthLimitsSource pattern (apps/collector, registered through the existing builder registry): an OpenAI/Codex subscription limits source and a Z.ai coding-plan limits source; both are undocumented endpoints, so every failure degrades gracefully to LimitsUnavailableError plus a data_quality_events record instead of failing collection (FR-LIMIT-013); credentials are read locally and never uploaded, matching the Anthropic source. Gateway-observed rate-limit headers ingestible as optional snapshots through POST /api/v2/ingest/limits (FR-LIMIT-010). Forecasting identifies its source data and confidence (FR-LIMIT-008); unknown windows never require schema migrations (FR-LIMIT-009).

Acceptance criteria (PRD Epic TOK-12): window registry describes kinds, labels, and periods; snapshots support account, organization, and source dimensions; the existing Anthropic OAuth source keeps working unchanged; OpenAI/Codex and Z.ai sources report subscription windows; gateway rate-limit headers ingestible; dashboards and alerts read window metadata from the registry.

Test strategy: unit tests for the registry and grouping rules; collector unit tests with recorded endpoint fixtures for both new sources including failure degradation; integration test for gateway snapshot ingest; migration tests for the new limit_snapshots columns; regression test that existing Anthropic window history and alerts remain intact.

subtasks.title: Provider window registry
subtasks.description: Window kinds, labels, and period semantics per provider, exposed via the API.
subtasks.details: Add window metadata to the provider registry (packages/core descriptor plus providers table supported_dimensions or a dedicated windows JSON column): each provider declares its window kinds with display label, period semantics (rolling duration, calendar reset, or opaque), and sort order; Anthropic seeds five_hour, seven_day, seven_day_opus, seven_day_sonnet with the labels currently hardcoded in the dashboard; storage keeps window_kind fully opaque (FR-LIMIT-001) and unknown kinds never require migrations (FR-LIMIT-009), rendering with the raw kind as fallback label. Expose through GET /api/v2/providers (extending 61.5) so dashboards and alerts resolve labels dynamically (FR-LIMIT-012). Test strategy: unit tests for registry seed content and fallback labeling of unknown kinds; API test asserting window metadata in provider responses; contract test that the dashboard label helper (Task 67.9 consumer) resolves all seeded Anthropic kinds to the exact current labels, guaranteeing zero visual change for existing deployments.

subtasks.title: limit_snapshots dimensions migration and grouping rules
subtasks.description: Account, organization, and source dimensions with no-merge semantics.
subtasks.details: Alembic migration extending limit_snapshots: account (nullable string label), organization (nullable), source_id (nullable reference), limit_amount Numeric nullable, remaining Numeric nullable, unit (nullable string like tokens, requests, percent) alongside the existing utilization_pct, resets_at, provenance, and raw payload (FR-LIMIT-002/003); existing rows migrate with null new dimensions. Service-layer grouping rule: limit streams are keyed by (provider, window_kind, account, organization, source scope) and different keys are never merged into one series without an explicit configured grouping rule (FR-LIMIT-005); official and estimated snapshots stay distinguishable in every query (FR-LIMIT-004). Update the v2 limits ingest endpoint (62.7) to persist the full dimension set, removing its transitional feature flag. Test strategy: migration tests both engines with existing-data preservation; grouping-rule unit tests (two accounts same window produce two series); ingest round-trip with full dimensions; v1 limits ingest regression (dimension-less snapshots keep working, golden suite green).

subtasks.title: OpenAI/Codex limits source in the collector
subtasks.description: Subscription-window polling for Codex plans with graceful degradation.
subtasks.details: Add apps/collector limits_openai.py implementing a LimitsSource for OpenAI/Codex subscription windows, modeled on AnthropicOAuthLimitsSource: read local Codex CLI credentials (auth.json under the Codex home directory, access token only, never uploaded), call the usage or rate-limit endpoint the Codex client uses, map the response to LimitSnapshot rows with provider openai, provider-defined window kinds (primary and secondary rolling windows as reported), utilization, resets_at, account label from the local account id, provenance official; every failure path (missing credentials, endpoint change, auth expiry, network) raises LimitsUnavailableError so collection degrades gracefully and emits a limit_source_failure data quality event server-side via the standard limits upload (FR-LIMIT-011/013). Register under sources key openai_codex in the collector builder registry with enabled default false. Because the endpoint is undocumented, isolate all endpoint specifics in one module with recorded fixtures so drift is a one-file fix (R-008 pattern). Window kinds get registry entries (69.1) with labels. Test strategy: unit tests against recorded response fixtures covering healthy, malformed, expired-auth, and missing-credential cases; degradation test asserting the collector loop continues and other sources still upload; no-token-leak test asserting uploaded payloads never contain the access token.

subtasks.title: Z.ai coding-plan limits source in the collector
subtasks.description: GLM coding-plan window polling with graceful degradation.
subtasks.details: Add apps/collector limits_zai.py implementing a LimitsSource for Z.ai GLM coding-plan quotas, same pattern as 69.3: read the locally configured Z.ai API key or coding-plan credential (from the standard Z.ai client config location, configurable path), call the quota or usage endpoint the Z.ai coding plan exposes, map to LimitSnapshot rows with provider zai, provider-defined window kinds (the plan quota windows as reported, for example prompt-count per five hours), utilization, resets_at, provenance official; all failures raise LimitsUnavailableError with server-side limit_source_failure data quality recording (FR-LIMIT-011/013); credentials never leave the machine. Register under sources key zai_coding_plan, enabled default false, endpoint specifics isolated in one module with recorded fixtures. Registry window entries with labels (69.1). Test strategy: unit tests on recorded fixtures for healthy, malformed, quota-exhausted, and missing-credential responses; degradation and no-leak tests mirroring 69.3; collector config round-trip test enabling both new sources together with anthropic_oauth.

subtasks.title: Gateway rate-limit header snapshots
subtasks.description: Proxy-observed provider rate limits as optional estimated snapshots.
subtasks.details: Support gateways submitting observed rate-limit headers as limit snapshots (FR-LIMIT-010): document the mapping in the proxy integration contract (anthropic-ratelimit-* and x-ratelimit-* header families to window kinds like requests_per_minute, tokens_per_minute with limit_amount, remaining, resets_at), provenance estimated (headers are point-in-time observations, FR-LIMIT-004), source identity attached so gateway-observed and collector-official streams stay separate under the no-merge rule; per-window-kind rate limiting of snapshot ingest (accept at most one snapshot per source per window kind per configurable interval) so high-traffic gateways do not flood the table. Registry entries for the API rate-limit window kinds with labels. Extend the conformance fixtures (65.7) with a limits batch. Test strategy: integration test posting header-derived snapshots through /api/v2/ingest/limits with an ingest:limits token asserting provenance, dimensions, and flood-control behavior; documentation review of the header mapping table; no-merge verification with simultaneous collector and gateway streams for the same provider.

subtasks.title: Limit forecasting with source and confidence labeling
subtasks.description: Exhaustion prediction across providers with explicit data provenance.
subtasks.details: Generalize the existing burn-rate and exhaustion prediction (services/analytics.py) to any provider window with period semantics from the registry: prediction identifies its source data (which limit stream and which usage series) and a confidence level derived from sample density and window age (FR-LIMIT-008); subscription windows without utilization history yield unavailable rather than a guess (FR-DIM-010 semantics); multi-account streams predict independently (FR-LIMIT-005). The /summary/now v1 response keeps its current Anthropic-shaped fields (golden suite) while the v2 limits endpoint (66.6) gains forecast objects per stream. Test strategy: unit tests for period-semantics math across rolling and calendar windows, confidence derivation, insufficient-data behavior, and per-account independence; regression test that Anthropic five-hour predictions match current outputs on identical fixtures; integration test with all three providers seeded.

subtasks.title: Limits epic tests and documentation
subtasks.description: Cross-provider verification and operator docs for the new sources.
subtasks.details: Epic verification: end-to-end test seeding limit streams for anthropic (collector official), openai (collector official), zai (collector official), and a gateway-estimated stream, asserting the v2 limits API returns all streams with correct registry labels, provenance, and forecasts, and that the dashboard limit pages (Task 67.9) render them; existing Anthropic limit history, blocks, and alerts unchanged (golden suite plus alert regression). Documentation: docs/collector/overview.md gains setup sections for openai_codex and zai_coding_plan sources (credential locations, config keys, poll intervals, failure behavior); docs/architecture/ gains a limits-v2 section covering the window registry, dimensions, grouping, and forecasting; deploy/collector.example.toml updated with the new source blocks disabled by default. Epic acceptance walkthrough of PRD Epic TOK-12 and AC-020 in the task log. Test strategy: the end-to-end suite green on both engines; docs review against FR-LIMIT-001..013; example TOML validated by the collector config parser in a test.

tasks.title: TOK-10: Retention, Security, and Operations
tasks.description: Production hardening: configurable incremental retention with rollup verification, snapshot compaction, administrative deletion APIs, audit log, rate limiting and request bounds, backup and restore automation, runbooks, security and privacy test suite, and release gates. PRD-TOK-002 Sections 12.17, 12.18; Epic TOK-10; FR-PRIV-001..012, FR-RET-001..007, NFR-SEC-001..008; decisions D-010, D-014.
tasks.details:
Objective: safe long-term operation of the v2 platform (Migration Phase 6).

Design: retention configuration per record category with PRD defaults (raw final attempts 180 days, superseded snapshots 7 days per D-010, daily rollups indefinite, limit snapshots 400 days, ingest batch metadata 30 days, audit records 400 days, corrections indefinite); retention jobs incremental and resumable (FR-RET-002) with rollup verification before raw deletion (FR-RET-004, FR-ROLLUP-010) and legal hold support (FR-RET-006); consistent behavior on SQLite and Postgres (FR-RET-007); operational retention status visible (FR-RET-005). Administrative deletion by source, machine, project, or time range (FR-PRIV-007) recorded in audit_log alongside repricing, token operations, and price applies (FR-PRIV-009, NFR-SEC-005). Rate limiting split between ingest and query (FR-INGEST-015, NFR-SEC-003); request size and JSON depth limits (NFR-SEC-004); restrictive CORS defaults and TLS deployment guidance (NFR-SEC-006/007). Backup and restore automation with restore tests (NFR-REL-004); runbooks for migration and rollback, token rotation, and upgrade (FR-SEC-009, AC-018). Security and privacy test suite: prohibited-key fuzzing (FR-PRIV-012), oversized metadata, deeply nested JSON, token scope bypass, revoked tokens, WebSocket authorization, secret redaction, pseudonymized identifiers, SQL and filter injection (PRD 18.6). Stored-field documentation for privacy review (FR-PRIV-005). Tenancy is out of scope but schema review confirms nothing precludes it (D-014).

Acceptance criteria (PRD Epic TOK-10): configurable retention exists; backup and restore tests pass; scoped token rotation documented; rate limits and request bounds enforced; audit log covers administrative actions; migration and rollback runbooks exist; performance and security gates pass.

Test strategy: unit tests for retention eligibility; integration tests for retention jobs on production-scale fixtures with resume-after-interrupt; restore verification on SQLite and Postgres; the full security and privacy suite; sustained-load performance test at the documented 1000 events per second reference target (NFR-PERF-002); trivy clean; final walkthrough of release acceptance criteria AC-001 through AC-022.

subtasks.title: Retention configuration model
subtasks.description: Per-category retention policy with legal hold.
subtasks.details: Add a retention policy model (settings plus app_settings overrides): per-category durations with PRD 12.18 defaults (raw final attempt events 180 days, superseded snapshot revisions 7 days per decision D-010, daily rollups indefinite, limit snapshots 400 days, ingest batch metadata 30 days, security audit records 400 days, administrative correction records indefinite, alert events 400 days); category-level enable and disable plus a global legal-hold flag that suspends all deletion (FR-RET-001/006); the usage_events_v1_archive table from 62.10 becomes a policy category (default retained until operator opt-in deletion). Policy validation rejects nonsensical values (raw events shorter than rollup verification lag). Expose GET and PUT /api/v2/admin/retention requiring admin:retention scope, audited. Test strategy: unit tests for defaults, override precedence, validation rules, and legal-hold gating; API scope and audit tests.

subtasks.title: Retention jobs: incremental, resumable, verified-before-delete
subtasks.description: Background deletion with rollup verification and snapshot compaction.
subtasks.details: Implement the retention worker (background loop like the alert engine): per category, delete in bounded batches (configurable, default 5000 rows) oldest-first with a persisted high-water mark so interrupted runs resume without rescanning (FR-RET-002); raw attempt events are only deleted after verifying the covering daily rollups exist and match event sums for the affected days (FR-RET-004, FR-ROLLUP-010; mismatch aborts the day, records a data quality event); superseded snapshot revisions compact after 7 days; referential integrity maintained by deleting dependents first (computed_costs and billable_units rows with their events; FR-RET-003); consistent behavior on SQLite and Postgres including vacuum guidance (FR-RET-007). Retention status (per-category last run, rows deleted, oldest retained, pending backlog) exposed operationally via readiness or an admin endpoint (FR-RET-005). Test strategy: integration tests on production-scale seeded tables (PRD 18.5) covering batch progression, resume-after-kill, verification-failure abort, legal-hold suspension, and integrity (no orphaned cost or unit rows); dual-engine runs.

subtasks.title: Administrative deletion APIs
subtasks.description: Targeted deletion by source, machine, project, or time range.
subtasks.details: Add DELETE (POST-based for safety) /api/v2/admin/data endpoints for targeted administrative deletion by source, machine, project, or time range (FR-PRIV-007): dry-run mode returning affected row counts per table first, then an explicit confirm call referencing the dry-run digest (mirroring the pricing import pattern); deletion cascades through usage rows, cost records, billable units, revisions, and optionally rollup recomputation for affected days; every execution writes an audit_log entry with actor, criteria, digest, and per-table counts (FR-PRIV-009); admin:retention scope required; legal hold blocks execution. This is the GDPR-style and mistake-recovery surface for a privacy owner (persona 6.5). Test strategy: integration tests for each criterion including dry-run accuracy versus actual deletion, digest mismatch rejection, rollup recomputation correctness after deletion, audit completeness, and legal-hold blocking; scope denial tests.

subtasks.title: audit_log table wired to every administrative action
subtasks.description: One auditable trail for corrections, repricing, imports, tokens, retention, deletions.
subtasks.details: Alembic migration creating audit_log (id, ts, actor token label or bootstrap, action, subject, detail JSONType, request_id) indexed on ts and action; services/audit.py record() helper called from every administrative path: correction ingest (62.4), repricing and revert (64.6), price imports and rate mutations (64.9/64.10), token create, revoke, rotate (63.5), source revocation and edits (63.5), retention policy changes (70.1), administrative deletions (70.3), and rollup rebuilds; entries are append-only with no delete API and 400-day default retention as a policy category; secrets never appear in detail payloads (NFR-SEC-005, FR-PRIV-009/011). GET /api/v2/admin/audit with filters for review, admin scope. Test strategy: per-action integration assertions that exactly one audit row with correct fields is written (folded into each surface test via a shared fixture); redaction test injecting token-like strings into action paths; migration tests; append-only API test.

subtasks.title: Rate limiting, request bounds, and transport hardening
subtasks.description: Ingest and query rate classes, size and depth limits, CORS and TLS posture.
subtasks.details: Harden the API surface: finalize the rate limiter started in 62.6 into configurable per-token-class limits distinguishing ingest and query traffic (FR-INGEST-015, NFR-SEC-003) with 429 plus Retry-After responses matching the client backoff contract; global request-size middleware and JSON depth enforcement across all endpoints, not only v2 ingest (NFR-SEC-004); restrictive CORS defaults (same-origin dashboard only, configurable allowlist, NFR-SEC-007); TLS-required guidance and secure-headers middleware for public deployment documented in docs/deployment/server.md (NFR-SEC-006); WebSocket connection limits per token. Configuration through Settings with env and app_settings override, secrets stay secret-managed (NFR-SEC-002). Test strategy: integration tests for both rate classes with Retry-After correctness, oversized and over-deep request rejection on representative v1 and v2 endpoints, CORS preflight behavior for allowed and denied origins, and WS connection capping; load-shaped test that ingest limiting does not affect query availability.

subtasks.title: Backup and restore automation with restore verification
subtasks.description: Automated, tested backups on both engines.
subtasks.details: Extend deploy/backup.sh and add restore tooling: scheduled logical backups (pg_dump for Postgres, sqlite3 .backup for SQLite) with retention rotation aligned to the documented policy (FR-PRIV-010); an automated restore-verification script that restores the latest backup into a scratch database, runs Alembic head check, executes integrity queries (row counts per table, checksum over daily rollups), and reports; wired as a CI job against fixtures and as a deployable cron unit for production (NFR-REL-004, AC-014); documented recovery-time expectations. Backups run before every migration per the baseline constraint (60.5). Test strategy: end-to-end backup-and-restore test on both engines inside CI with data seeded, corruption-detection test (tampered backup fails verification), and a documented manual restore drill recorded in the runbook; shellcheck clean with header blocks per shell standards.

subtasks.title: Operational runbooks and retention visibility
subtasks.description: Migration, rollback, rotation, and upgrade runbooks; retention status surfaced.
subtasks.details: Write docs/operations/ runbooks: migration runbook (pre-flight backup, maintenance window, Alembic upgrade, verification queries, the 62.8 verifier, go and no-go criteria), rollback runbook (Alembic downgrade boundaries, which migrations are irreversible and what restore-from-backup covers, AC-018), token rotation runbook (scoped replacement flow from 63.5, FR-SEC-009), and upgrade runbook (server, collector, and proxy version compatibility matrix with schema_version negotiation, NFR-REL-005). Surface retention and operational status in the dashboard Settings or Fleet area (last retention run, backup age, migration head) using existing view conventions. Failure modes and operational metrics documented per the Definition of Done. Test strategy: runbooks walked through end to end against a staging copy (documented evidence in the task log); retention status API and UI tests; docs review for completeness against AC-013/014/018.

subtasks.title: Security and privacy test suite
subtasks.description: The full adversarial suite from PRD 18.6.
subtasks.details: Consolidate and extend the adversarial tests into a dedicated suite (apps/server/tests/security/): prohibited content keys across every metadata extension point (reusing and extending the 62.2 fuzz corpus); oversized metadata and deeply nested JSON on all ingest endpoints; token scope bypass attempts (each scope against each endpoint class, forged and truncated tokens); revoked token behavior including in-flight WebSocket disconnection; WebSocket authorization edge cases; secret redaction in logs and API responses (grep-based log scanning in tests, FR-INGEST-017, FR-PRIV-011); pseudonymized identifier operation (exporter-side hashed machine and project names flow end to end, FR-PRIV-004); SQL and filter injection attempts through every query filter parameter (PRD 18.6). Document all stored fields for privacy review in docs/architecture/ (FR-PRIV-005). Test strategy: the suite itself is the deliverable; it runs in the standard CI gate; any finding is fixed or tracked with a high-priority follow-up before this subtask closes per the quality-gate rule; coverage of every 18.6 bullet demonstrated by a checklist mapping tests to requirements.

subtasks.title: Performance and security gates with release acceptance walkthrough
subtasks.description: Sustained load, trivy, and the AC-001..AC-022 checklist.
subtasks.details: Final gates for general availability: sustained ingest load test at 1000 events per second on documented reference hardware using the 66.8 harness (NFR-PERF-002, AC-015) with results in docs/architecture/performance.md; retention job execution on production-scale tables during load (PRD 18.5); trivy filesystem and image scans with zero HIGH or CRITICAL findings (NFR-SEC-001, AC-016); dependency audit for the dashboard and clients. Execute the full release acceptance checklist AC-001 through AC-022 from PRD Section 19 against a staging deployment fed by real collector data and the proxy harness, recording evidence per criterion in the task log and a release notes document; any failed criterion spawns a blocking follow-up task. Test strategy: this subtask is the acceptance execution itself; done means every AC has recorded passing evidence, both gates are green, and the epic parent plus program can close.

tasks.title: TOK-11: OpenTelemetry Interoperability and OTLP Bridge
tasks.description: Align v2 events with OpenTelemetry generative AI semantic conventions: trace and span linkage, a pinned-version mapping document, and a feature-flagged OTLP receiver that converts GenAI spans into v2 attempt events with content attributes stripped. PRD-TOK-002 Section 12.19; Epic TOK-11; FR-OTEL-001..007; decisions D-009, D-013.
tasks.details:
Objective: OpenTelemetry interoperability including the receiver bridge, feature-flagged off by default (D-009).

Design: trace_id, span_id, and parent_span_id on the v2 schema (FR-OTEL-001) including agent parent-child relationships (FR-TRACE-009); a mapping document pinning a specific generative AI semantic-convention version (D-013) covering provider, model, token usage, latency, and outcome mappings (FR-OTEL-002); the pinned semconv version recorded on every mapped event (FR-OTEL-006). OTLP receiver: HTTP protobuf endpoint, disabled by default behind a feature flag, authenticated consistently with ingest tokens, converting GenAI spans into v2 attempt events with provenance imported and source type sdk; content attributes stripped unconditionally (FR-OTEL-007); unknown attributes retained in namespaced extra metadata within bounds. Core v2 ingest never depends on OTLP (FR-OTEL-004); export to external OpenTelemetry backends stays deferred (FR-OTEL-005).

Acceptance criteria (PRD Epic TOK-11): trace and span IDs supported; mapping document exists with pinned semconv version; content attributes remain disabled by default; the feature-flagged OTLP receiver converts generative AI spans into v2 attempt events (AC-021).

Test strategy: unit tests for the span-to-event converter including content stripping and attribute bounding; integration test posting recorded OTLP payloads from an instrumented sample app and asserting correct v2 events and dashboard visibility; feature-flag-off test proving the endpoint is absent by default; privacy fuzz tests on span attributes.

subtasks.title: Trace and span linkage with agent hierarchies
subtasks.description: Verify and complete trace_id, span_id, parent_span_id support end to end.
subtasks.details: Complete the OTel identity plumbing started in the 62.1 schema: trace_id, span_id, and parent_span_id validated as W3C trace-context format when present (lenient passthrough for non-conforming ids with a data quality note), indexed for lookup, exposed through attempt query responses, and usable to reconstruct agent parent-child relationships (FR-OTEL-001, FR-TRACE-008/009): attempts sharing a trace_id form a trace group; parent_span_id links subagent attempts to their parent attempt for the sessions and agents display (67.6 consumer). Add trace_id filtering to /api/v2/attempts. Test strategy: unit tests for format validation and lenient fallback; integration test ingesting a parent-child attempt pair via the proxy wire and asserting trace grouping and parent resolution in query responses; index presence check.

subtasks.title: Semantic-convention mapping document with pinned version
subtasks.description: The normative GenAI semconv mapping (decision D-013).
subtasks.details: Write docs/architecture/otel-mapping.md: pin the exact OpenTelemetry generative AI semantic-convention version implemented (verify the current stable release at implementation time against opentelemetry.io semconv docs; record it as a constant in code); mapping table from semconv attributes to v2 fields (gen_ai.system to provider via alias normalization, gen_ai.request.model to requested_model, gen_ai.response.model to native_model, gen_ai.usage.input_tokens and output_tokens to counters, cache and reasoning token attributes where conventions define them with provider-extension fallback to extra.otel, error.type and span status to outcome and success, span start and end to ts_started and ts_completed, server latency derivation, gen_ai.operation.name handling); document unmapped v2 concepts (finality, sequence, billing modes) and their defaults for span-derived events; content attributes (gen_ai.prompt, gen_ai.completion and event bodies) are listed as unconditionally stripped (FR-OTEL-002/006/007). The semconv_version is recorded on every mapped event in extra.otel.semconv_version. Test strategy: documentation review against the pinned semconv release notes; the mapping table drives the 71.4 converter tests one to one (each row becomes a test case).

subtasks.title: Feature-flagged OTLP receiver service
subtasks.description: Authenticated OTLP/HTTP endpoint, off by default.
subtasks.details: Add an OTLP/HTTP receiver to the server (apps/server services/otel_receiver.py plus api/v2/otlp.py): POST /api/v2/otlp/v1/traces accepting protobuf and JSON-encoded OTLP ExportTraceServiceRequest payloads (opentelemetry-proto dependency only, no SDK or collector infrastructure per NG-005 and FR-OTEL-004); entirely behind a feature flag (settings otlp_enabled default false; when disabled the route is not mounted, G-012); authenticated with the standard bearer tokens requiring ingest:events scope; size and depth limits shared with ingest hardening; spans that are not GenAI spans (no gen_ai.system attribute) are acknowledged and skipped with a counter. Partial-success responses follow OTLP semantics. Test strategy: integration tests for flag-off 404, flag-on auth enforcement, protobuf and JSON payload parsing, non-GenAI span skipping, and OTLP partial-success response shape; trivy and dependency review for the proto dependency.

subtasks.title: Span-to-v2 converter with content stripping
subtasks.description: GenAI spans become v2 attempt events, privacy enforced.
subtasks.details: Implement the converter from GenAI spans to v2 attempt events per the 71.2 mapping: event_id derived deterministically from trace_id plus span_id (stable replay identity, FR-IDEMP-001), event_kind attempt, finality final (spans arrive complete), provenance imported, source type sdk with the OTLP client identity, semconv_version recorded; provider resolved through alias normalization with unknown providers following registry policy; token counters from usage attributes with absent values as zero and unknown usage attributes preserved in extra.otel within bounds; content attributes stripped unconditionally before any persistence and covered by the privacy validator as a second layer (FR-OTEL-007, defense in depth); converted events flow through the standard ingest service gaining privacy validation, revision semantics, source registration, cost computation, and rollups like any other source. Test strategy: one test per mapping-table row; property fuzz test embedding gen_ai.prompt and completion content asserting nothing content-like reaches storage; replay test (same span batch twice is a no-op); unknown-provider span policy test.

subtasks.title: End-to-end OTLP verification with an instrumented sample
subtasks.description: Prove a real instrumented app lands correctly in the dashboard.
subtasks.details: Build a minimal sample application (scripts/otel_sample/ or a test fixture) instrumented with the OpenTelemetry SDK emitting GenAI spans for mock LLM calls across two providers, exporting OTLP/HTTP to the receiver; end-to-end assertions: events appear in /api/v2/attempts with correct provider, models, tokens, latency, and trace linkage; usage reaches rollups and the dashboard renders it under the OTLP source with source type sdk; cost computation prices known models and marks unknown ones unpriced (the unpriced workflow from PRD 18.4 exercised via OTLP); content attributes verifiably absent from every stored row (AC-021 evidence). Record the walkthrough in the task log. Test strategy: the e2e suite in CI using recorded OTLP payloads from the sample (deterministic), plus a documented manual run of the live sample against the dev stack; dashboard verification via the 67 conventions.

subtasks.title: OTel documentation and epic closure
subtasks.description: User-facing OTLP docs and the deferral boundary.
subtasks.details: Write docs/integrations/opentelemetry.md: enabling the receiver (feature flag, token scope, endpoint), supported semconv version and the mapping summary linking to the architecture doc, content-attribute policy (always stripped, FR-OTEL-007), configuration examples for common SDK exporters, limitations (traces only, no metrics or logs; finality always final; no OTLP export from Tokemetry to external backends per FR-OTEL-005 deferral), and troubleshooting (flag off, scope missing, non-GenAI spans skipped). Update the website docs API page reference if applicable. Epic acceptance walkthrough of PRD Epic TOK-11 criteria and AC-021 in the task log; confirm the receiver stays off by default in a fresh deployment test. Test strategy: docs review against FR-OTEL-001..007; fresh-deployment default-off test; full suite green closing the epic and, with all other epics done, the program.
